alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models using Random Forests with the top 5% of protein biomarkers demonstrated reasonable accuracy (0.707) and area under the curve (0.714) for COPD prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The same modeling claim is preserved but the reported performance metrics differ between preprint and publication, indicating revision of numeric results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mass spectrometry proteomic analysis of lung tissue is a promising approach for the identification of biomarkers for COPD. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No explicit affirmative statement about the approach being 'promising' appears in the publication; the broader methodological endorsement was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: COVID-19 vaccination rates play a crucial role in determining future COVID-19 disease risk. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: COVID-19 vaccination rates play a crucial role in determining future COVID-19 disease risk. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and certainty in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Higher vaccination rates significantly correlated with lower COVID-19 case growth predictions beginning in mid-March 2021. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Higher vaccination rates significantly correlated with lower COVID-19 case growth predictions beginning in mid-March 2021. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The temporal correlation and statistical direction are reported unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: As the relative importance of COVID-19 vaccination data features grew, demographic SDOH features such as age, race, and ethnicity decreased in impact. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: As the relative importance of COVID-19 vaccination data features grew, demographic SDOH features such as age, race and ethnicity decreased in impact. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The relative feature-importance shift is presented in the same terms and certainty in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The impact of socioeconomic and environmental factors, including access to healthcare and transportation, increased as vaccination data features became more important. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The impact of socioeconomic and environmental factors, including access to healthcare and transportation, increased as vaccination data features became more important. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Content and emphasis on socioeconomic/environmental factors are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating a data framework to track evolving patterns of community-level SDOH risk factors could improve health equity and resilience to future public health emergencies. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating a data framework to track community-level SDOH risk factors could improve health equity and resilience to future public health emergencies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The recommendation is present in both versions but the publication expresses it with increased certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The adaptive testing cohorts produced positivity rates that were 26% higher than the random cohort. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The data-driven COVID-19 testing program produced a positivity rate of 0.53% from 20,862 tests. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports an overall absolute positivity rate rather than the relative 26% increase stated in the preprint; the metric and framing were revised and the relative-comparison claim was not preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Within 14 days of their selection, 2.94% of the adaptive cohort tested positive, compared to 1.27% of the random cohort. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The positivity rate of students testing positive within five days of the initial test was 1.49%, which is a significant increase from the general surveillance baseline of 0.67%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports positivity over a different (5-day) window with different rates and baseline; the specific 14-day comparison figures were revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Close contacts predicted by the adaptive testing models received a COVID-19 test within an average of 0.94 days of the source testing positive. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Close contacts predicted by the data-driven models were tested much more quickly on average (0.94 days) than those who were manually contact traced (1.92 days). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The rapid testing interval for predicted close contacts is reported identically and the publication adds a direct comparison to manual tracing without changing the original finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning strategies can improve surveillance testing effectiveness in a university setting. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study suggests that other universities and organizations could adopt similar strategies to help quickly identify positive cases and reduce community transmission. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the conclusion as a practical recommendation for adoption, clarifying applicability while preserving the original inference about improved surveillance effectiveness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automatic seizure onset zone (SOZ) localization using interictal electrocorticogram (ECoG) improves the diagnosis and treatment of patients with medically refractory epilepsy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automatic seizure onset zone (SOZ) localization using interictal electrocorticogram (ECoG) improves the diagnosis and treatment of patients with medically refractory epilepsy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The high-level clinical conclusion is identical in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates that significant coupling at band pairs of slow waves and high frequency oscillations (HFOs) exists in the SOZ when compared with the non-seizure onset zone (NSOZ). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A significant coupling at band pairs of slow waves and high frequency oscillations (HFOs) exists in the SOZ when compared with the non-seizure onset zone (NSOZ). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about significant coupling is reproduced verbatim between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed machine learning models effectively classify PAC features to identify SOZ. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The phase-amplitude coupling (PAC) features and the proposed machine learning models achieve better classification performance for identifying the SOZ. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies that PAC features and models achieve better classification performance but reduces the certainty level, tempering the original stronger claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of novel feature-wise/class-wise re-weighting strategies improves the classification performance of the models. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Novel feature-wise/class-wise re-weighting strategies were introduced to handle imbalanced dataset classification. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports introduction of the re-weighting strategies but tones down the explicit claim that they improve performance, focusing instead on their role addressing imbalance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 4.8% of patients identified with dementia had recorded suicidal ideation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 15.1% of patients had recorded suicidal ideation, which was more common in dementia with Lewy bodies compared to other dementia diagnoses studied.
alignments.rationale: Both claims report prevalence of recorded suicidal ideation but the reported percentage is substantially different in the publication, so the result has been revised rather than unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recorded suicidal ideation was almost twice as common in dementia with Lewy bodies compared to other dementia diagnoses studied.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 15.1% of patients had recorded suicidal ideation, which was more common in dementia with Lewy bodies compared to other dementia diagnoses studied.
alignments.rationale: Both statements say suicidal ideation was more common in DLB, but the publication drops the quantitative 'almost twice' phrasing and states the association more generally, a tempered/clarified claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dementia with Lewy bodies, physical illnesses or disability, and neuropsychiatric symptoms were significantly associated with suicidal ideation after adjusting for sociodemographic and clinical factors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Suicidal ideation was more frequent in those with depression and dementia with Lewy bodies and less common in those with impaired activities of daily living and in vascular dementia.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a different pattern of associations (highlighting depression and reduced frequency with impaired ADLs/vascular dementia), so the original list of significant associations has been revised and narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mini-Mental State Examination score at diagnosis was not associated with suicidal ideation.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not report any finding about MMSE score and suicidal ideation, indicating this specific result is absent from the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Suicidal ideation is present in sufficient proportions of people with dementia to justify treatment of potentially modifiable risk factors within dementia care pathways.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings highlight the importance of identifying and treating depressive symptoms in people with dementia.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's general recommendation to treat modifiable risk factors has been narrowed in the publication to specifically emphasize identifying and treating depressive symptoms.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of SEEG electrodes in a brain-computer interface (BCI) for recording and decoding neural signals related to movement and touch is viable.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study demonstrates the first decoding of sulcal and subcortical activity related to both movement and tactile sensation in the human hand using SEEG.
alignments.rationale: The publication more specifically frames the result as the first demonstration of sulcal/subcortical decoding with SEEG, clarifying and tightening the preprint's viability claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining temporal correlation-based feature selection with LSTM recurrent neural networks yielded decoding accuracies of up to 92.04% for hand movements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed algorithm based on temporal correlation and deep learning methods achieved decoding accuracies of up to 92.04 ± 1.51% for hand movements.
alignments.rationale: Both statements report the same high decoding accuracy with essentially the same methodological description; the publication adds a confidence interval but otherwise matches the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Repeating features were found in sulcal, gyral, and white matter areas and were predominantly phasic or phasic-tonic across a wide frequency range.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study demonstrates the first decoding of sulcal and subcortical activity related to both movement and tactile sensation in the human hand using SEEG.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes decoding from sulcal/subcortical sites which overlaps the preprint's anatomical observations, but it does not report the same detailed feature temporal/frequency descriptions, so the claim is less directly preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings may lead to a new class of minimally invasive brain-computer interface systems, increasing applicability to a wider variety of conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings suggest that minimally invasive brain-computer interface systems could be developed, increasing applicability to a wide variety of conditions.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is essentially the same, but the publication assigns lower certainty to this forward-looking claim (publication labels it low certainty), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A deep-learning model trained with an external image data set can be used to extract image features and improve the classification accuracy of a data set that does not contain enough image data to train a deep-learning model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A deep-learning model trained with an external image data set can be used to extract image features and improve the classification accuracy of a data set that does not contain enough image data to train a deep-learning model.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this methodological/result claim verbatim, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified the most important clinical features for ER patient classification through clinical feature importance analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An analysis of clinical feature importance was performed to identify the most important clinical features for ER patient classification.
alignments.rationale: The core claim is the same, but the publication downgrades the stated certainty (medium vs high in preprint), so confidence is slightly tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can be upgraded to include a SARS-CoV-2 specific classification with COVID-19 patients data.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention plans or conclusions about upgrading the model for SARS-CoV-2/COVID-19 classification, so this preprint future-extension claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models based on non-interpretable features outperformed interpretable ones, especially in cross-lingual experiments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models based on non-interpretable features outperformed interpretable ones, especially in cross-lingual experiments.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this core comparative performance result directly and quantitatively, preserving the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Among the non-interpretable features used, TRILLsson provided the most stable and accurate results across tasks and data sets.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention TRILLsson by name or report this specific claim about that feature set, so the preprint claim about TRILLsson is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Interpretable feature-based models provide insights into speech and language deterioration.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These results suggest that interpretable feature-based models can be used by clinicians to evaluate the deterioration of the speech of patients with PD.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the interpretable-model insight claim more concretely as a clinical application for evaluating speech deterioration, but with lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Non-interpretable feature-based models can be used to achieve higher detection accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Non-interpretable feature-based models can be leveraged to achieve higher detection accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same conclusion but with slightly lower certainty (publication labels it medium), so the claim content is preserved but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both interpretable and non-interpretable features displayed robust behaviors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: For interpretable feature-based models, the mean of the best F1-scores obtained was 81% in mono-lingual, 81% in multi-lingual, and 71% in cross-lingual experiments.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides quantitative performance breakdowns for interpretable features (and for non-interpretable features in another claim), which clarifies the preprint's general statement about robust behavior.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Horizon-scanning for innovative technologies can help prepare regulators for earlier access to medical products.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Citation network analysis and text mining can be used for horizon scanning of AI-based medical technologies.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies methods (citation network analysis and text mining) for horizon-scanning, clarifying the preprint's general conclusion about horizon-scanning usefulness for regulators.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Citation network analysis and text mining were proposed and validated as methods for horizon-scanning in the field of AI-based medical image analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Citation network analysis and text mining can be used for horizon scanning of AI-based medical technologies.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same methodological claim that these techniques can be used for horizon scanning; the wording shifts from 'proposed and validated' to an affirmative usage statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study classified 119,553 publications into 36 clusters, providing insights into the academic landscape of AI applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study classified 119,553 publications into 36 clusters, demonstrating the academic landscape of AI applications.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this exact quantitative result and its interpretation of mapping the academic landscape.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI-based electrocardiograms and electroencephalograms can lead to the development of innovative medical products.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent research trends in AI-based medical technologies include topics such as ECG/EEG, human activity recognition, and drug discovery.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's causal claim (can lead to product development) is softened in the publication to an observation that ECG/EEG are among current research trends, a narrower and less causal framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Significant research progress can be detected through a quick increase in constituent papers and citations in the clusters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Significant research progress in AI-based medical technologies is indicated by a quick increase in constituent papers and citations of hub papers.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this detection signal for research progress with nearly identical wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Explicit task structural knowledge determines human use of model-based reinforcement learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Explicit task structural knowledge is a primary determinant of model-based reinforcement learning in humans. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core claim with slightly different phrasing ('primary determinant'), which clarifies emphasis but keeps the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Providing task structure information strongly increased model-based control across all groups. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Providing task structure information strongly increased model-based control across all groups studied. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result language and meaning are effectively identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Initial behaviour was model-free, with rewards directly reinforcing preceding actions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Initial behaviour in the decision-making task was model-free, with rewards directly reinforcing preceding actions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same empirical observation with minimal wording change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model-based control emerged with experience in a minority of subjects, and less in individuals with OCD. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model-based control emerged with experience in a minority of participants and less in those with obsessive-compulsive disorder. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about emergence and reduced prevalence in OCD is preserved in meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Eating disorders are associated with one of the highest mortality rates among all mental disorders. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication's presented claims do not restate the high-mortality claim; it appears omitted from the set of highlighted publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Body image dissatisfaction significantly reduces model-based learning in individuals with disordered eating compared to healthy controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a significantly reduced model-based contribution in the body image dissatisfaction task condition in the population characterized by high scores of disordered eating compared to a healthy control. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core empirical result in equivalent terms about reduced model-based contribution in the target population.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study investigates the trade-off between model-based and model-free learning in the context of body image dissatisfaction. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims emphasize findings and implications rather than restating the study's investigational aim, so this preprint contribution is not explicitly represented.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings suggest general deficits in deliberate control in individuals with body image dissatisfaction, leading to habitual, compulsive-like behaviors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This interpretive conclusion about general deficits and habitual behaviors is present in the publication but was not explicit as a claim in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The results may inform treatment approaches that focus on enhancing reliance on goal-directed decision making to help cope with unwanted behaviors. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a treatment-relevant implication that was not stated as a preprint claim, so it is treated as an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DTSCs serve as an effective platform for acquiring multimodal datasets necessary for developing remote screening methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DTSCs serve as an effective platform for acquiring multimodal datasets necessary for developing remote screening methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The acquisition setup using thermal, infrared, and RGB cameras, along with microphones, enables contactless estimation of physiological parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The acquisition setup includes an array of thermal, infrared, and RGB cameras, as well as microphones, for contactless estimation of physiological parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The described acquisition setup and its purpose match closely between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recording multimodal datasets during the early stages of a pandemic can facilitate and speed up the identification of infection-specific symptoms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recording a multimodal dataset of DTSC participants can facilitate and speed up the identification of infection-specific symptoms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about facilitating identification of symptoms via early multimodal recording is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Large scale data acquisition at DTSC can be achieved without disturbing the flow of operation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Large-scale data acquisition at DTSC can be achieved without disturbing the flow of operation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The practical result about non-disruptive large-scale acquisition is retained verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are strong correlations between the true grades and the predicted values for both yellow color grade and neointimal coverage grade. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are strong correlations between the true grades and the predicted values for both yellow colour grade and neointimal coverage grade. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is the same claim with only orthographic differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The binary classification model for the red thrombus achieved an F1-score of 0.71 ± 0.03 and an area under the ROC curve of 0.91 ± 0.02. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The binary classification model for the red thrombus achieved an F1-score of 0.71±0.03 and an area under the receiver operator characteristic curve of 0.91±0.02. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance metrics and meaning are identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The standard GAN model could generate realistic CAS images with an average Inception score of 3.57 ± 0.06. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The standard GAN model could generate realistic CAS images with an average Inception score of 3.57±0.06. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The generative modeling result is carried over verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GAN-based data augmentation improved the performance of the prediction models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GAN-based data augmentation improved the performance of the prediction models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about augmentation improving prediction performance is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DCNN is useful in both predictive and generative modeling for developing a diagnostic support system for CAS. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DCNN is useful in both predictive and generative modelling that can help develop the diagnostic support system for CAS. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The high-level conclusion about DCNN utility is preserved with only minor wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent findings from X-Ray imaging techniques suggest that such images contain relevant information about the SARS-CoV-2 virus. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Application of Deep Neural Network (DNN) techniques coupled with radiological imaging can be helpful in the accurate identification of COVID-19 disease. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication moves from a tentative statement about image information to a stronger, actionable claim about DNN+imaging aiding accurate identification, broadening scope and increasing confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our proposed approach provides a classification accuracy of 97.36%, 97.65% of sensitivity, and a precision of 99.28%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed VGG-16 Network-based Faster Regions with Convolutional Neural Networks (Faster R-CNN) framework provides a classification accuracy of 97.36%, 97.65% sensitivity, and 99.28% precision. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance numbers are identical; the publication specifies the network architecture but does not change the empirical claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We believe this proposed method might be of assistance for health professionals to validate their initial assessment towards COVID-19 patients. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method might be of assistance for health professionals to validate their initial assessment towards COVID-19 patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The content of the assistance claim is the same, but the publication reports it with higher certainty (low -> medium).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Participants had nearly equal trust in AI versus physician diagnoses, but were significantly more likely to trust an AI diagnosis of cancer over a doctor's diagnosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Participants had nearly equal trust in AI versus physician diagnoses.
alignments.rationale: The preprint statement about nearly equal trust maps directly to Q0; the core claim about equal trust is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Participants had nearly equal trust in AI versus physician diagnoses, but were significantly more likely to trust an AI diagnosis of cancer over a doctor's diagnosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Participants were significantly more likely to trust an AI diagnosis of cancer over a doctor's diagnosis when the survey suggested that an AI could make medical diagnoses.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the cancer-specific finding but adds contextual wording about the survey framing; the substantive result is the same but the publication clarifies the condition under which it was observed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 55% of respondents reported they were uncomfortable with automated robotic surgery, yet 88% mistakenly believed that partially autonomous surgery was already being performed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 55% of respondents reported that they were uncomfortable with automated robotic surgery.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's first statistic (55% uncomfortable) is reported identically in Q2, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 55% of respondents reported they were uncomfortable with automated robotic surgery, yet 88% mistakenly believed that partially autonomous surgery was already being performed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The majority of individuals surveyed mistakenly believed that partially autonomous surgery was already happening.
alignments.rationale: Both statements report the same phenomenon (misconception about autonomous surgery); the publication generalizes the preprint's 88% figure to 'the majority,' slightly altering specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Almost all participants (94%) stated they would be willing to pay for an AI to review their medical imaging, if available.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 94% of participants stated that they would be willing to pay for a review of medical imaging by an AI if available.
alignments.rationale: The percentage and willingness-to-pay claim are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most participants express confidence in AI providing medical diagnoses, sometimes even over human physicians.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Participants had nearly equal trust in AI versus physician diagnoses.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's concluding phrasing is a generalization of the result reported in Q0; the publication frames this as near-equal trust, which clarifies the underlying data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Health care providers should be cognizant of patient misconceptions and the sensitivity that patients have to how AI technology is represented.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims are all results and do not include this prescriptive conclusion, so the recommendation is not present in the publication claims list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Digital health technologies (DHTs) have a highly visible potential for harnessing in clinical and public health practices in Ethiopia.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Digital health technologies (DHTs) have a highly visible potential for harnessing in clinical and public health practices in Ethiopia.
alignments.rationale: The claim is verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The adoption and implementation of DHTs in Ethiopia require more training, access to better devices, and improved infrastructure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The adoption and implementation of DHTs in Ethiopia require more training, access to better devices, and improved infrastructure.
alignments.rationale: The recommendation about training, devices, and infrastructure is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DHTs hold significant promise for addressing major clinical and public health backlogs and strengthening the healthcare ecosystem in Africa.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DHTs hold significant promise for addressing major clinical and public health backlogs and strengthening the healthcare ecosystem in Ethiopia.
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the geographic scope from 'Africa' to 'Ethiopia,' revising the scope while keeping the core optimistic conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: More randomized controlled trials (RCTs) are needed on emerging DHTs to provide robust evidence of their potential use in clinical and public health settings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: More randomized controlled trials (RCTs) are needed on emerging DHTs to provide robust evidence of their potential use in Ethiopia.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the need for more RCTs but limits the recommendation to Ethiopia rather than broader settings, representing a narrowed scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methodological quality of all eligible studies was rated as critically low.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methodological credibility of all eligible studies was rated as critically low, with 95% of papers having significant flaws in reporting quality.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the 'critically low' assessment and adds additional detail about reporting flaws, thereby clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 91% of papers had significant flaws in reporting quality.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methodological credibility of all eligible studies was rated as critically low, with 95% of papers having significant flaws in reporting quality.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a higher proportion (95% vs 91%) of papers with reporting flaws, strengthening (amplifying) the reporting-quality claim and revising the specific figure.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Almost three fourths of the studies included less than 10% of available studies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Almost three-quarters of the studies included less than 10% of available studies.
alignments.rationale: The wording and substance of this coverage metric are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Due to the low credibility of evidence and flawed reporting, any recommendation about automated COVID-19 clinical diagnosis from medical images using AI at this point cannot be provided.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include this categorical conclusion about withholding recommendations, so the preprint's explicit recommendation-withholding is not present in the publication claims list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best multimodal model achieved an AUC of 0.88, while the best radiomics model achieved an AUC of 0.68.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best multimodal model achieved an AUC of 0.88, indicating strong predictive performance for malignancy in follicular thyroid neoplasms.
alignments.rationale: The AUC results are identical and the publication frames the 0.88 AUC as strong performance, consistent with the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inclusion of topological features yields strong improvement over radiomics-based features alone in the prediction of follicular carcinoma on ultrasound.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inclusion of topological features yields strong improvement over radiomics-based features alone in the prediction of follicular carcinoma on ultrasound.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about topological features improving performance is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TDA features explicitly capture shape information that likely augments the performance of the multimodal machine learning model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TDA features explicitly capture shape information that likely augments the performance of the multimodal machine learning model.
alignments.rationale: The rationale about TDA features capturing shape information is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A quantitative based ultrasound risk stratification system may contribute to the preoperative prediction of follicular carcinoma.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A quantitative based ultrasound risk stratification system may contribute to the preoperative prediction of follicular carcinoma.
alignments.rationale: The tentative suggestion about a quantitative risk stratification system is carried forward unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MRI-based measures can predict neurodevelopmental outcomes in neonates with hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MRI-based measures can predict neurodevelopmental outcomes in neonates with hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy.
alignments.rationale: The predictive claim is present in both the preprint and publication with consistent wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The correlation of MRI-based measures with Bayley scores is more than twice that of correlations based on only clinical data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predictions based on MRI-based measures showed greater correlations with Bayley scores than those based solely on demographic and laboratory data.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the direction of the comparison but softens the numeric magnitude ('more than twice' -> 'greater correlations'), clarifying/toning down the specific effect size.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MRI-based measures explain more than four times the variance in observed Bayley scores compared to clinical data alone.
alignments.rationale: The specific quantitative claim about explaining 'more than four times the variance' does not appear among the publication claims, so this precise numeric claim is not preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of clinical and MRI-based measures provides similar or marginally better predictions of neurodevelopmental outcomes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of demographic-laboratory and MRI-based measures generated similar or marginally better predictions of neurodevelopmental outcomes.
alignments.rationale: Both versions state that combining data types yields similar or marginally improved predictions; wording is effectively the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our novel equations outperformed existing equations for each of the body composition parameters measured. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our novel equations outperformed existing equations for each of the body composition parameters measured. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The wording and assertion are effectively identical between preprint and publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean absolute error for total body fat mass was significantly lower using our novel equations compared to existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean absolute error for total body fat mass was significantly lower using our novel equations compared to existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result claim is restated verbatim in the publication with the same certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed equations may provide improved estimation of body composition in research and clinical contexts conducted in India. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Supplementing body composition estimates from BIA devices with simple anthropometric measures can greatly improve the validity of BIA-measured body composition in South Asians. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the improvement as specifically achieved by supplementing BIA with anthropometrics and refers to South Asians rather than only India, clarifying mechanism and broadening geographic scope while increasing certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The equations will be made available as an online application for use in future research in these populations. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our equations are made available for use by other researchers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication indicates the equations are already made available (rather than future provision), increasing certainty while preserving the contribution's intent.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach could be extended to other BIA devices and populations to improve the performance of BIA devices. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This extension/suggestion is present in the publication but was not asserted in the preprint, representing an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A model including all omics and clinical variables yielded a cross-validated receiver operator characteristic area under the curve (ROCAUC) of 0.84. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed prediction model including all omics and clinical variables achieved a cross-validated receiver operating characteristic area under the curve (ROCAUC) of 0.84. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is restated in the publication with equivalent metrics and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The IMI DIRECT prediction models out-performed existing non-invasive NAFLD prediction tools. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The IMI DIRECT prediction models outperformed existing noninvasive NAFLD prediction tools. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is carried forward unchanged in wording and implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We have developed clinically useful liver fat prediction models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of omics and clinical variables yielded the highest accuracy in predicting liver fat accumulation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the accuracy achieved by combining data types rather than explicitly labeling the models 'clinically useful', so the contribution is reframed in terms of measured accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Biological features that appear to affect liver fat accumulation have been identified. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clinical variables demonstrated better prediction ability than complex omics variables. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts emphasis to clinical variables outperforming omics, which revises the preprint's broader claim about identifying biological features and narrows the inferred role of omics; this also tempers the implication about biological feature importance.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study's findings may not translate well to individuals of other ancestries or those exposed to different environmental risk factors. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit limitation about generalizability that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Random Forest (RF) algorithm is the most suitable for the classification of Cushing’s Syndrome (CS). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Random Forest (RF) algorithm is the most suitable for the classification of Cushing's Syndrome (CS). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about RF being most suitable is reproduced verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach can classify CS subjects with an average accuracy of 92% and an average F1 score of 91.5%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach can classify CS with an average accuracy of 92% and an average F1 score of 91.5%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Metrics and claim are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RF-based one vs. all binary classification model achieves a sensitivity of 97.6%, precision of 91.1%, and specificity of 87.1% to discriminate CS from non-CS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RF-based one vs. all binary classification model achieves a sensitivity of 97.6%, precision of 91.1%, and specificity of 87.1% to discriminate CS from non-CS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance metrics are unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RF-based multiclass classification model achieves an average per class sensitivity of 91.8%, average per class specificity of 97.1%, and average per class precision of 92.1% to classify different subtypes of CS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RF-based multiclass classification model achieves an average per class sensitivity of 91.8%, average per class specificity of 97.1%, and average per class precision of 92.1% to classify different subtypes of CS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The multiclass performance claim is carried forward without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed models can help improve physician’s judgment in diagnosing Cushing’s Syndrome. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed models can help improve physicians' judgment in diagnosing Cushing's Syndrome. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim about clinical utility is preserved with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A multi-stage satellite-based machine learning model was developed to estimate daily fine particulate matter (PM 2.5) levels across Great Britain during 2008-2018. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A multi-stage satellite-based machine learning model was developed to estimate daily fine particulate matter (PM2.5) levels across Great Britain between 2008-2018. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is restated in the publication with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The random forest architecture performed well in all stages, with Stage-3 showing an average cross-validated R² of 0.767 and minimal bias. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The random forest architecture performed well in all stages, with Stage-3 showing an average cross-validated R2 of 0.767 and minimal bias. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance metrics and interpretation are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrated better performance over the temporal scale compared to the spatial component, achieving R² values of 0.795 and 0.658, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieved good accuracy with an R2 of 0.795 for the temporal scale and 0.658 for the spatial component. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same performance split between temporal and spatial components with equivalent metrics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The high spatio-temporal resolution and precision of the dataset allows it to be used in epidemiological analyses to assess health risks associated with PM 2.5 exposure. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The high spatio-temporal resolution and precision of the estimates allow for their use in epidemiological analyses to assess health risks associated with PM2.5 exposure. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the data's suitability for epidemiological use with increased certainty and slightly stronger phrasing, clarifying confidence in applicability.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Direct satellite observations must be integrated with other satellite-based products and geospatial variables to derive reliable estimates of air pollution exposure. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit methodological recommendation about integrating data sources that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using cognitive, fluorodeoxyglucose-positron emission tomography or electroencephalography and magnetoencephalography variables significantly improved predictive performance compared to not including them. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using cognitive, fluorodeoxyglucose-positron emission tomography, or potentially electroencephalography and magnetoencephalography variables significantly improved predictive performance compared to not including them. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication hedges the role of EEG/MEG with 'potentially', slightly tempering the original unqualified claim while keeping the core finding about cognitive and FDG-PET variables.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Including other modalities, in particular T1 magnetic resonance imaging, did not show a significant effect on predictive performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Including other modalities, in particular T1 magnetic resonance imaging, did not show a significant effect on predictive performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The finding about T1 MRI's lack of added predictive value is restated without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The good performance of cognitive assessments questions the wide use of imaging for predicting the progression to Alzheimer's disease. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The good performance of cognitive assessments questions the wide use of imaging for predicting the progression to Alzheimer's disease and advocates for exploring further fine domain-specific cognitive assessments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the critique of imaging use but expands the recommendation to explore finer domain-specific cognitive assessments, broadening the conclusion's implications.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several methodological issues were identified, including the absence of a test set or its use for feature selection or parameter tuning in nearly a fourth of the papers. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Several methodological issues were identified, including the absence of a test set or its use for feature selection or parameter tuning in nearly a fourth of the papers. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The methodological limitation is reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Short-term predictions are likely not to be better than predicting that subjects stay stable over time. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Short-term predictions are likely not to be better than predicting that subjects stay stable over time. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about short-term predictive limits is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed metrics and associated statistics enable users to rapidly examine representativeness of all subpopulations in RCTs with respect to target populations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed metrics and associated statistics enable users to rapidly examine representativeness of all subpopulations in the RCT defined by a set of categorical traits.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same capability but adds the detail that subpopulations are defined by categorical traits, a small clarification rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The normalized metrics provide an intuitive standardized scale for evaluating representation across subgroups in RCT study cohorts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The normalized metrics provide an intuitive standardized scale for evaluating representation across subgroups.
alignments.rationale: The core claim about normalized metrics providing an intuitive standardized scale is preserved; the publication omits the explicit phrase "in RCT study cohorts" but meaning remains the same in context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods can support generalizability evaluation of existing RCT cohorts and enrollment target decisions for new RCTs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methods can support generalizability evaluation of existing RCT cohorts by quantifying the gaps between RCT and target populations.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains support for generalizability evaluation but omits the explicit claim about informing enrollment target decisions for new RCTs, narrowing the scope of the original contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The metrics are beneficial complements to other approaches used to identify generalizability and health equity of RCTs.
alignments.rationale: No publication statement corresponds to the claim that the metrics complement other approaches for generalizability and health equity; the publication instead adds a different claim about an interactive visualization tool.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The symbolic regression methodology developed an equation utilizing the methylation levels of three biomarkers, achieving an accuracy of 91.3%, a sensitivity of 100%, and a specificity of 87.5% on the test data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The symbolic regression methodology developed an equation utilizing the methylation levels of three biomarkers, achieving an accuracy of 91.3%, a sensitivity of 100%, and a specificity of 87.5% on the test data.
alignments.rationale: The result metrics and method are reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Circulating cell-free DNA presents opportunities for minimally invasive early diagnosis of hepatocellular carcinoma.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Circulating cell-free DNA presents opportunities for minimally invasive early diagnosis of hepatocellular carcinoma.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about cfDNA opportunities is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Utilizing transparent machine learning approaches like symbolic regression can allow accurate diagnosis by combining biological and mathematical principles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Utilizing transparent machine learning approaches like symbolic regression can allow accurate diagnosis by combining biological and mathematical principles.
alignments.rationale: The methodological conclusion is carried over without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future validation of the model obtained here on a larger and more diverse dataset can reveal the potential for such approaches in cancer diagnosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Future validation of the model on a larger and more diverse dataset can reveal the potential for such approaches in cancer diagnosis.
alignments.rationale: The limitation/next-steps statement is present with equivalent meaning in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis of feedback from 37,914 users identified three major themes of issues with the NHS Test & Trace Service.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Results from analysis of feedback by 37,914 users highlighted issues with the Service falling within three major themes.
alignments.rationale: The result about three major themes from the same user feedback set is restated equivalently.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Structural Topic Modelling coupled with thematic analysis was found to be an effective technique to rapidly acquire user insights.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Structural Topic Modelling coupled with thematic analysis was found to be an effective technique to rapidly acquire user insights.
alignments.rationale: The methodological conclusion is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Topic modelling can be a quick and cost-effective method to provide high quality, actionable insights from free-text feedback.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Topic modelling can be a quick and cost-effective method to provide high quality, actionable insights from free-text feedback to optimize public health Services.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the application to optimizing public health services, which clarifies intended use and narrows the generality of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A deep learning algorithm based on fundus photographs achieved an AUC of 0.944 in the internal validation group for identifying individuals with high dementia risk.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm based on fundus photographs achieved an AUC of 0.944 in the internal validation group and 0.926 in the external group for identifying individuals with high dementia risk.
alignments.rationale: The publication consolidates internal and external AUCs into one statement; the internal validation result is preserved while the claim is presented alongside the external performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm also achieved an AUC of 0.926 in the external validation group for identifying individuals with high dementia risk.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm based on fundus photographs achieved an AUC of 0.944 in the internal validation group and 0.926 in the external group for identifying individuals with high dementia risk.
alignments.rationale: The publication merges the external AUC with the internal AUC into a single result sentence; the external result itself is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The estimated CAIDE dementia risk score derived from the algorithm was significantly associated with both comprehensive cognitive function and specific cognitive domains.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The estimated CAIDE dementia risk score derived from the algorithm was significantly associated with both comprehensive cognitive function and specific cognitive domains.
alignments.rationale: The association between the estimated CAIDE score and cognitive measures is reported identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm has potential to be utilized as a noninvasive and more expedient method for dementia risk stratification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm trained via fundus photographs has the potential to be utilized as a non-invasive and more expedient method for dementia risk stratification.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the conclusion about potential noninvasive risk stratification using the algorithm.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm might be adopted in dementia clinical trials to efficiently select eligible participants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This algorithm might be adopted in dementia clinical trials to efficiently select eligible participants based on dementia risk.
alignments.rationale: The content is substantively the same, but the publication lists this claim with a higher indicated certainty (medium vs low), representing an amplification of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning model achieved a sensitivity of 0.889 and specificity of 0.889 at the image level for DME detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning model achieved a sensitivity of 0.889, specificity of 0.889, and an AUC of 0.954 at the image level.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the reported sensitivity and specificity and adds AUC information, clarifying performance without changing the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: At the eye level, the model achieved a sensitivity of 0.905 and specificity of 0.902 for DME detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: At the eye level, the model achieved a sensitivity of 0.905, specificity of 0.902, and an AUC of 0.964.
alignments.rationale: The eye-level sensitivity/specificity are unchanged and the publication augments the claim with AUC, a clarifying additional metric.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrated a sensitivity of 0.901 and specificity of 0.900 at the patient level for DME detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: At the patient level, the model achieved a sensitivity of 0.900, specificity of 0.900, and an AUC of 0.962.
alignments.rationale: Patient-level metrics are essentially the same (minor rounding) and the publication adds the AUC, clarifying overall performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DME can be detected from color fundus imaging with high performance on all analysis metrics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Diabetic macular edema can be detected from color fundus imaging with high performance on all analysis metrics.
alignments.rationale: The general conclusion about high performance detection from fundus imaging is preserved verbatim aside from expanded acronym.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automatic DME detection may simplify screening, leading to more encompassing screening for diabetic patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automatic DME detection may simplify screening, leading to more encompassing screening for diabetic patients.
alignments.rationale: The translational implication about simplification and broader screening is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Loss of ellipsoid zone, pigment epithelium detachment, subretinal fluid, and Drusen were the most abundant features in the cohort.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Detection and measures of loss of ellipsoid zone and retinal pigment epithelium were more variable.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts emphasis from these features being 'most abundant' to their detection being more variable, i.e., the claim was changed rather than retained verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The features subretinal fluid, intraretinal fluid, hypertransmission, descent of the outer plexiform layer, and pigment epithelium detachment showed highest inter-reader agreement.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Subretinal fluid, intraretinal fluid, hypertransmission, descent of the outer plexiform layer, and pigment epithelium detachment showed highest interreader agreement.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result with essentially identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inter-reader agreement depended on the respective OCT-based feature.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The interreader agreement depended on the respective OCT-based feature.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is carried over verbatim with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A selection of reliable features might provide suitable surrogate markers for disease progression and possible treatment effects focusing on different disease stages.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A selection of reliable features might provide suitable surrogate markers for disease progression and possible treatment effects focusing on different disease stages.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this conclusion with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This might give opportunities to a more time- and cost-effective patient assessment and improved decision-making as well as have implications for clinical trials and training machine learning algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this broader, lower-certainty implication present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 34% of enrolled patients demonstrated olfactory loss on psychophysical testing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Thirty-four percent of enrolled patients demonstrated olfactory loss on psychophysical testing.
alignments.rationale: The numeric result is identical and retained in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most ML methods performed favorably in terms of predictive ability compared to traditional logistic regression.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most ML methods performed favorably in terms of predictive ability compared to traditional logistic regression.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same comparison and conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several actionable features were identified as risk factors for CRS-OD.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Several actionable features were identified as risk factors for CRS-OD.
alignments.rationale: The identification of actionable risk features is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ML methods may be useful for current understanding and future study of hyposmia secondary to sinonasal disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ML methods may be useful for current understanding and future study of hyposmia secondary to sinonasal disease.
alignments.rationale: The text is the same but the publication increases the stated certainty from low to medium, indicating amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MALDI-TOF MS can classify and predict the severity of COVID-19 in a clinical setting.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MALDI-TOF MS has potential as a bench to bedside technology to assist clinicians in decision making for COVID-19 patients.
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserted clinical classification ability; the publication reframes this more cautiously as potential to assist clinicians, reducing the strength/scope of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a clear variability of the serum peptidome profile depending on COVID-19 severity, with 78 peaks significantly different between critical and mild patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The serum peptidome profile varies significantly depending on COVID-19 severity.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core result (peptidome varies with severity) but omits the specific count of 78 peaks, making the statement less detailed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning approaches classified severe and non-severe COVID-19 patients with 90% accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning approach classified clinical stable patients according to their severity with 100% accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The performance metrics and the described target group differ between versions (90% for severe vs non-severe in preprint vs 100% for clinically stable patients in publication), so the claim was revised and reported accuracy increased.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning predicted the favourable outcome of severe patients in 85% of cases and the unfavourable outcome in 38% of cases.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report these specific outcome-prediction performance figures; the claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Five proteins, including serum amyloid proteins A1 and A2, were significantly upregulated in critical COVID-19 patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Five proteins were identified as significantly upregulated in critical COVID-19 patients, including serum amyloid protein A2.
alignments.rationale: Both versions identify five upregulated proteins; the publication explicitly mentions A2 but omits explicit mention of A1, a slight clarification/reduction in detail rather than full removal.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning techniques can detect abnormal left ventricular geometry even before the onset of left ventricular hypertrophy in a population without established cardiovascular disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning techniques can detect abnormal left ventricular geometry even before the onset of left ventricular hypertrophy in a population without established cardiovascular disease.
alignments.rationale: The primary result is carried over verbatim with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieved an accuracy of 86%, specificity of 75%, sensitivity of 95%, and an area under the receiver operating curve of 0.89 when distinguishing normal geometry from all other groups.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieved 87% accuracy, 75% specificity, and 97% sensitivity in distinguishing normal geometry from concentric remodeling and left ventricular hypertrophy combined.
alignments.rationale: Performance numbers and the exact grouping are changed in the publication (slightly different accuracy/sensitivity and explicit grouping), so the claim is revised rather than identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model also classified normal geometry and concentric remodeling against those with established left ventricular hypertrophy with an accuracy of 89%, specificity of 97%, sensitivity of 50%, and an area under the receiver operating curve of 0.85.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model also achieved 89% test set accuracy, 93% specificity, and 67% sensitivity in classifying normal geometry and concentric remodeling against those with left ventricular hypertrophy.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports different specificity and sensitivity values while keeping the same classification task; metrics were revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hypertension, age, body mass index over the Sokolow-Lyon voltage, QRS-T angle, and QTc duration were identified as some of the most important features for detecting abnormal left ventricular geometry.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hypertension, age, body mass index over the Sokolow-Lyon voltage, QRS-T angle, and QTc duration were identified as some of the most important features for classification.
alignments.rationale: The set of important features is preserved with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Innovative solutions are needed to improve risk stratification of patients without established cardiovascular disease, and machine learning may enable this direction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Innovative solutions are needed to improve risk stratification of patients without established cardiovascular disease, and machine learning may enable progress in this direction.
alignments.rationale: The concluding recommendation is essentially the same, with a minor wording change that does not alter meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Average percentages of elective procedures generally decreased from 100% to 60% in relation to the number of surgical claims and the number of DRGs among them.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cross-validated multiple regression explained 73.9% of variation in average percent elective admissions among hospitals.
alignments.rationale: The preprint describes a descriptive trend; the publication emphasizes a regression model explaining variation — related but the publication reframes the result in modeling terms, reducing direct correspondence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some providers with high volumes of claims had anomalously low percentages of elective procedures (5% – 40%).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The exceptionally low average percent elective admissions of 96 hospitals could not be explained by surgical claims volume, surgical DRGs among claims, or hospital characteristics.
alignments.rationale: The publication quantifies and analyzes these low-elective hospitals (96) and reports inability to explain them by measured factors, which clarifies/extends the preprint observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These low elective outliers were not explained by the particular surgical DRGs among their claims.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The exceptionally low average percent elective admissions of 96 hospitals could not be explained by surgical claims volume, surgical DRGs among claims, or hospital characteristics.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states that surgical DRGs (among other factors) did not explain the outliers, matching the preprint conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Among hospitals exempted from PSIs, those with the greatest volume of claims were always low elective outliers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These outlier hospitals were disproportionately exempt from elective-based Patient Safety Indicator scores.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the relationship in reciprocal terms (outliers were disproportionately exempt), which clarifies but does not materially contradict the preprint's observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some hospitals with relatively high numbers of surgical claims may have classified procedures as non-elective in a way that ultimately exempted them from certain PSIs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some hospitals may have classified admissions in a way that exempted them from elective-based Patient Safety Indicator scores.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core conclusion that classification practices may have led to exemptions from PSI scoring.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Transparency into admission classification policies is needed to ensure fair and reliable use of Patient Safety Indicators when ranking hospitals and adjusting payments.
alignments.rationale: This policy/recommendation about transparency appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: No statistically significant unfairness was found in the composition of the dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: No statistically significant unfairness was found in the composition of the dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result verbatim with the same certainty, so there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Varying degrees of unfairness were identified among modalities, with no single modality consistently demonstrating better fairness across all demographic variables. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Varying degrees of unfairness were identified among modalities, with no single modality consistently demonstrating better fairness across all demographic variables. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim text is the same but the publication lowers reported certainty, so the substance is unchanged while confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Post-training mitigation effectively improved both DPR and EOR metrics at the expense of a decrease in F1 scores. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Post-training mitigation effectively improved both DPR and EOR metrics at the expense of a decrease in F1 scores. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same quantitative result with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Addressing and mitigating unfairness in these automated tools are essential steps in fostering trust among clinicians. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Addressing and mitigating unfairness in these automated tools are essential steps in fostering trust among clinicians. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about fostering clinician trust is reproduced unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Proper unfairness reporting and mitigation of these tools is the first step to building trust among clinicians and patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the explicit claim referencing both clinicians and patients and explicit phrasing about 'first step', so this preprint claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the GCNMLP on the SIDER, OFFSIDERS, and FAERS datasets is superior to the non-negative matrix factorization method and some well-known machine learning methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GCNMLP method demonstrates superior performance compared to the non-negative matrix factorization method and other well-known machine learning methods on the SIDER, OFFSIDERS, and FAERS datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same comparative performance claim with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: New side effects of drugs can be obtained using the GCNMLP. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GCNMLP can uncover new side effects of drugs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim that GCNMLP can identify new drug side effects is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The GCNMLP enables better drug side effect prediction, which improves personalized medicine prescriptions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel in silico method using GCNMLP will shorten the time spent in uncovering unseen side effects within routine drug prescriptions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the translational impact from a broad improvement to personalized prescriptions to a more specific operational benefit (shorter time to uncover side effects), so the conclusion has been revised and narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Approximately 29% of the 2858 youth in our study population had preDM/DM based on biomarker criteria. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Approximately 29% of the 2858 youth in our study population had preDM/DM based on biomarker criteria. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric prevalence result is reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The clinical guideline had a sensitivity of 43.1% and specificity of 67.6% in identifying preDM/DM status among youth. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The clinical guideline had a sensitivity of 43.1% and specificity of 67.6%, indicating poor performance in identifying preDM/DM status among youth. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same operating characteristics but explicitly interprets those numbers as indicating poor performance, thus clarifying the implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some machine learning-based classifiers performed comparably to or better than the screening guideline in identifying preDM/DM youth. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some machine learning-based classifiers performed comparably to or better than the screening guideline in identifying preDM/DM youth. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same comparative performance claim for machine learning classifiers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the clinical guideline varied across demographic subgroups. (certainty=medium, type=result)
