alignments.rationale: The role of monitoring and PCR testing in early detection and screening is the same in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI-based on-court social-distancing monitoring revealed key time-dependent spatial metrics to define player-to-player proximity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI-based on-court social-distance monitoring revealed key time-dependent spatial metrics to define player-to-player proximity.
alignments.rationale: The AI-based monitoring finding is reproduced with equivalent wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Athletes regularly achieved around 80% of maximum HR during training, indicating anticipated improvements in achieving their physical intensities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Athletes regularly achieved around 80% of maximum heart rate during training, indicating anticipated improvements in achieving their physical intensities.
alignments.rationale: The heart-rate training intensity result is carried over unchanged from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The current challenge provided considerable know-how to create and manage a safe environment for high-performing athletes in the COVID-19 pandemic.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this reflective claim about gained know-how; instead it reports a different athlete-perception result, so the preprint claim was not retained.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Self-reported questionnaires revealed a sense of security among the athletes that allowed them to focus singularly on their training.
alignments.rationale: The athletes' self-reported sense of security is reported in the publication but was not present in the preprint, representing an added participant-perception finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most defacing methods were unable to produce any usable images for evaluation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most defacing methods were unable to produce any usable images for evaluation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result verbatim with the same certainty and implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model trained with original data had a statistically higher composite OAR Dice similarity coefficient (DSC) of 0.760 compared to the models trained with defaced data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model trained with original data had a statistically higher composite OAR DSC of 0.760 compared to the models trained with defaced data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the identical quantitative result and interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model trained with original data showed decreased performance when evaluated on defaced data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model trained with original data showed decreased performance when evaluated on defaced data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this performance observation with the same framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Defacing algorithms may have a significant impact on HNC OAR auto-segmentation model training and testing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Defacing algorithms may have a significant impact on HNC OAR auto-segmentation model training and testing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about potential impact is carried forward unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a need for further development of HNC-specific image anonymization methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a need for further development of HNC-specific image anonymization methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The recommendation for HNC-specific anonymization development is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI model can successfully identify CTPA exams with diagnostic interpretation limiting motion artifacts. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI model can successfully identify CTPA exams with diagnostic interpretation limiting motion artifacts. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The core conclusion about the model's ability is presented identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average performance of the AI model included 94% sensitivity, 91% specificity, 93% accuracy, and 0.93 area under the ROC curve. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The average performance of the AI model included 94% sensitivity, 91% specificity, 93% accuracy, and 0.93 area under the ROC curve. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance metrics are reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI model can help alert technologists about the presence of substantial motion artifacts on CTPA. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI model can help alert technologists about the presence of substantial motion artifacts on CTPA. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this applied contribution claim without change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Among the CTPA images from 793 patients, 421 had substantial motion artifacts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This patient-count result appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint; it is an added factual result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Radiology specific clinical decision support systems (CDSS) and artificial intelligence are poorly integrated into the radiologist workflow. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Radiology specific clinical decision support systems (CDSS) and artificial intelligence are poorly integrated into the radiologist workflow. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this high-level conclusion exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Radiologists have a high intent to use and a positive attitude towards the proposed conversational agent intervention. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The phase 1 survey identifies differences between average users and radiologist users in software interventions using the User Acceptance of Information Technology: Toward a Unified View (UTAUT) framework. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the survey findings as identification of differences rather than explicitly asserting high intent/positive attitude, so the original positive-attitude claim is tempered and narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Radiologist acceptance of software tools differs from the standard user as indicated by the findings from the User Acceptance of Information Technology (UTAUT) framework. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The phase 1 survey identifies differences between average users and radiologist users in software interventions using the User Acceptance of Information Technology: Toward a Unified View (UTAUT) framework. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication places the UTAUT-based difference-finding as a specific phase-1 survey result with higher reported certainty, clarifying and strengthening the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work contributes an understanding of how radiologist-users differ from the average user, which can be utilized by software developers to increase satisfaction of CDSS tools within radiology. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work contributes an understanding of how radiologist-users differ from the average user and can be utilized by software developers to increase satisfaction of CDSS tools within radiology. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the same contribution claim, though the stated certainty is slightly lower.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study develops a theory of radiologist-decision tool interaction using a sequential explanatory study design. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Phase 2 semi-structured interviews provide narratives on why these differences are found. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the sequential explanatory elements explicit (phase 2 interviews providing narratives), clarifying and strengthening the methodological claim from the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a novel solution called Radibot, a conversational agent capable of engaging clinicians with CDSS as an assistant using existing instant messaging systems. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The proposal of Radibot is a new, added contribution in the publication that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All models obtained a good Dice Similarity Coefficient (all DSC >= 0.928) for planning MRI. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: All models obtained a good Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) for planning MRI, with DSC values greater than or equal to 0.93. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication tightens the numeric threshold (0.928 -> 0.93), a small clarification in reported results while preserving the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MedNeXt trained with combined data was the best performing model for follow-ups at 3, 15 and 21 months. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MedNeXt trained with combined data provided the best DSC for follow-ups at 6, 15, and 21 months. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication changes one of the reported follow-up timepoints (3 months -> 6 months), revising the temporal detail of the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The addition of public data enhanced the performance of the models for follow-ups. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The addition of public data to the training dataset results in a statistically significant increase in performance in both models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the preprint's qualitative enhancement claim into a quantitatively supported, statistically significant result with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There was only limited difference in performance of the two algorithms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not assert the 'limited difference' conclusion; instead it emphasizes other comparative and temporal performance aspects, so this specific claim is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These algorithms hold promise as a valuable tool for clinicians for automated segmentation of pre- and postsurgical MRI. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These algorithms hold promise as a valuable tool for clinicians for automated segmentation of planning and follow-up MRI scans. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication uses slightly different terminology (planning/follow-up vs pre-/postsurgical) but preserves the same overall conclusion about clinical promise.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The models performed less effectively for follow-ups compared to planning MRI. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This comparative statement about follow-up vs planning MRI performance appears in the publication but was not in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EHRtemporalVariability is an Open Source R-package and Shiny-app designed to explore and identify temporal dataset shifts. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EHRtemporalVariability is an open-source R package and Shiny app designed to explore and identify temporal data-set shifts. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this software contribution with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EHRtemporalVariability demonstrates the capability to delineate dataset shifts in three impact case studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The capability of EHRtemporalVariability to delineate data-set shifts is demonstrated in three impact case studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The demonstration in three case studies is preserved exactly as a result in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EHRtemporalVariability enables the exploration and identification of dataset shifts, contributing to the repurposing of large, longitudinal datasets. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EHRtemporalVariability enables the exploration and identification of data-set shifts, contributing to the broad examination and repurposing of large, longitudinal data sets. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the phrasing slightly ('broad examination') but preserves the functional and repurposing conclusion, a clarification of scope rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The software is suited for both technical users and those not familiar with programming through its Shiny user interface. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state that the software is suited to non-programmers via Shiny in the provided claims, so this specific usability claim is not present.

alignments.pub_claim_text: EHRtemporalVariability estimates the statistical distributions of coded and numerical data over time and projects their temporal evolution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This explicit statement about estimating and projecting statistical distributions is present in the publication but was not in the preprint, so it is an added technical result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine learning-based risk score was developed and evaluated for predicting postoperative complications in the Portuguese cancer patient population.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning-based risk score was developed and evaluated for predicting postoperative complications in cancer patients.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the development/evaluation claim but drops the Portuguese-specific population, broadening the scope from a national cohort to cancer patients generally, so the claim content was revised and broadened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve (AUC) for predicting postoperative complications was 0.69.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive model achieved an AUC of 0.69 for the existence of postoperative complications and 0.74 for 1-year postoperative mortality.
alignments.rationale: The numerical AUC for postoperative complications is identical between preprint and publication and presented in the same context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean AUC for predicting one-year postoperative mortality was 0.74.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive model achieved an AUC of 0.69 for the existence of postoperative complications and 0.74 for 1-year postoperative mortality.
alignments.rationale: The reported AUC for 1-year postoperative mortality is unchanged and appears verbatim in the publication's combined result statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A web-based decision support system was provided to assist physicians in estimating cancer patients' risk of developing surgical complications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides a web-based decision support tool to assist physicians in clinical decision making.
alignments.rationale: Both texts describe provision of a web-based decision support tool for clinicians; the publication reframes it slightly in clinical decision-making terms but the contribution is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new multi-antigen serological test generates a full picture of seroconversion in a single reaction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new serological test generates a more complete picture of seroconversion than single antigen-based assays.
alignments.rationale: The publication tones the absolute "full picture in a single reaction" to a comparative statement ('more complete than single-antigen assays'), clarifying and narrowing the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most patients respond against all the viral antigens tested, while others show a marked bias to make antibodies against either proteins exposed on the viral particle or those released after cellular infection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most patients respond against all the viral antigens tested, while others show a marked bias to make antibodies against either proteins exposed on the viral particle or those released after cellular infection.
alignments.rationale: The result about heterogeneous antigen responses is identical in wording and meaning between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of all four antigens and machine learning techniques allows for clear discrimination between patients and healthy controls with 100% confidence.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The assay can discriminate between patients and healthy controls with 100% confidence.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the 100% discrimination claim; wording is slightly shorter but the performance claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-antigen assay improves the classification of patients with mild and severe disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Analyzing the response of multiple Ig isotypes to the four antigens may help establish a correlation with the severity degree of disease.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's assertive wording about improved classification is softened in the publication to a more tentative 'may help establish a correlation,' thus narrowing and tempering the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of this method will facilitate massive screenings of patients to evaluate their immune response.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not make the operational/consequential claim about facilitating massive screenings; this forward-looking implementation claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Actigraphy can clarify useful patient-centered outcomes for quantification of physical activity in the real-world setting.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Outpatient actigraphy provides information for 'real-world' patient-centered outcomes that are not captured by standardized respiratory questionnaires or lung function tests.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the general utility of actigraphy with additional detail about what it adds relative to questionnaires and lung tests, clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The activity, distance, and energy domains of actigraphy are closely correlated with each other but only loosely associated with spirometric and peak exercise measures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Actigraphy outputs are closely correlated with activity, distance, and energy domains but only loosely associated with spirometric and peak exercise measures.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding about correlations among actigraphy domains and weak association with spirometric/peak exercise measures.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sedentary domain of actigraphy is inversely associated with baseline lung volumes and tidal breathing at peak exercise.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The sedentary domain of actigraphy is inversely associated with baseline lung volumes and tidal breathing at peak exercise.
alignments.rationale: The result is the same but the publication expresses this with higher certainty (preprint medium vs publication high), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Outpatient actigraphy provides information for real-world patient-centered outcomes that are not captured by standardized respiratory questionnaires, lung function, or exercise testing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Outpatient actigraphy provides information for 'real-world' patient-centered outcomes that are not captured by standardized respiratory questionnaires or lung function tests.
alignments.rationale: This claim is essentially identical in both versions, emphasizing actigraphy's unique contribution to real-world outcomes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Seizures were accurately identified on the sub-scalp system, as confirmed by periods of concurrent conventional scalp EEG recordings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Seizures were accurately identified on the sub-scalp system, as visually confirmed by periods of concurrent conventional scalp EEG recordings.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication report the same verification of seizure identification by concurrent scalp EEG; wording differences are minimal.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The data acquired from the sub-scalp device allowed seizure forecasting to be successfully undertaken.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The data acquired from the sub-scalp device allowed seizure forecasting to be successfully undertaken.
alignments.rationale: The forecasting result is stated identically in publication and preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC score) achieved (0.88) is comparable to the best score in recent, state-of-the-art forecasting work using intracranial EEG.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC score) achieved was 0.88, which is comparable to the best score in recent, state-of-the-art forecasting work using intracranial EEG.
alignments.rationale: The numerical AUC and comparative claim are the same, but the publication presents the result with higher certainty (preprint medium vs publication high), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The procedures and devices were well tolerated, and no significant complications have been reported.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The procedures and devices were well-tolerated and no significant complications have been reported.
alignments.rationale: Tolerance and safety outcomes are reported the same way in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pulmonary function testing revealed restrictive physiology in the hospitalized and ICU groups but was normal in the ambulatory group.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the restrictive physiology finding; instead it emphasizes small airways disease and air trapping, so this specific PFT-based restrictive claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean percent of total lung classified as ground glass opacities (GGO) was significantly higher in hospitalized and ICU patients compared to ambulatory patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean percentage of total lung classified as ground-glass opacity (GGO) was significantly higher in hospitalized and ICU groups compared to the ambulatory group.
alignments.rationale: The GGO finding is reported identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Air trapping is present in patients with post-acute sequelae of COVID-19 and is independent of initial infection severity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Air trapping was significantly higher in all groups of participants with post-acute sequelae of COVID-19 compared to healthy controls.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the finding as elevated air trapping across all patient groups relative to healthy controls, which clarifies the preprint's statement about presence across severities rather than changing its substantive meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Air trapping measured by quantitative CT correlated with the residual volume to total lung capacity ratio (RV/TLC).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Air trapping correlated with the residual volume-to-total lung capacity ratio.
alignments.rationale: The correlation between air trapping and RV/TLC is reported the same way in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The concepts for COVID-19 positive cohort, COVID-19 index date, COVID-19 related admission, and the admission date were shown to have high values in all evaluation metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The concepts for COVID-19-positive cohort, COVID-19 index date, COVID-19-related admission, and the admission date demonstrated high values in all evaluation metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same evaluation-result claim with essentially identical wording and certainty, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The overall specificities, PPVs, and NPVs for the computed phenotypes were high, but sensitivities were relatively low. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Three phenotypes exhibited notable performance degradation compared to the positive predictive value in the prepandemic population, leading to their removal from the data mart. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint makes a broad statement about overall metrics and low sensitivities; the paper instead reports specific phenotype degradation and removal, narrowing and changing the emphasis on performance issues.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Participants expressed positive attitudes towards using DAPR for chart review, assessing the validation as easy and noting that DAPR helped find relevant information. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Participants in the survey expressed positive attitudes toward using the Digital Analytic Patient Reviewer (DAPR) for chart review, indicating that validation was easy and DAPR helped find relevant information. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same survey finding about participant attitudes and perceived usefulness of DAPR with comparable certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DAPR’s patient summary view accelerated the validation process. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of NLP technology in the chart review facilitated the COVID-19 data validation task and accelerated the process of providing reliable research data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication attributes acceleration to NLP-assisted chart review more broadly rather than specifically the patient-summary view, clarifying and broadening the mechanism while maintaining high confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The workflow for chart reviews using DAPR is in the process of being automated and will be extended to other domains. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DAPR's benefits can be expanded to other domains, and there are plans to operationalize it for wider research groups. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides more concrete wording about operationalization and expansion to wider research groups, clarifying and slightly broadening the original plan to extend and automate the workflow.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The early-warning model predicts in-hospital mortality for COVID-19 patients based on data obtained at the emergency department upon admission. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An early-warning model to predict in-hospital mortality for COVID-19 patients was developed and validated using a machine-learning approach. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both describe the same early-warning model predicting in-hospital COVID-19 mortality; the publication emphasizes development/validation with ML but the substantive claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The final score of the model is constructed using age, specific blood analytes, and the Brescia chest X-ray score. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Age and specific blood analytes were identified as the strongest predictors of in-hospital mortality in COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication highlights age and blood analytes as strongest predictors but omits explicit mention of the Brescia chest X-ray score as a model component, narrowing the described model composition.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The areas under the receiver operating characteristic curve for the training, validating, and testing groups were 0.98, 0.83, and 0.78, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieved areas under the ROC curve of 0.98, 0.83, and 0.78 for the training, validating, and testing groups, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the identical AUC results for the three cohorts, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model functions as a web-based calculator that provides an easy-to-interpret risk score. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model can be integrated into the Laboratory Information System of the hospital as a web-based death-risk calculator. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies integration into the hospital LIS as a web-based death-risk calculator, clarifying the deployment context of the web-based calculator claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can be used in the triage process to support decision-making on patient allocation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Among the 119,468 admissions, 10,693 (9.0%) developed acute kidney injury (AKI). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The AKI Risk Score (AKI-RiSc) achieved an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.730, outperforming an existing AKI Prediction Score model with an AUC of 0.665. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: At a cut-off of 4 points, AKI-RiSc had a sensitivity of 82.5% and specificity of 46.7%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: At a cut-off of 4 points, AKI-RiSc had a sensitivity of 82.6% and specificity of 46.7%. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AKI-RiSc is a simple point based clinical score that can be easily implemented for early identification of AKI in high-risk patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AKI-RiSc is a simple clinical score that can be easily implemented for early identification of AKI. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The START app can be administered by non-specialists to assess several domains of the autistic phenotype through direct observation and parent report. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study provides a proof of principle for START in digital screening of autism and related conditions in community settings. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We have identified a signature of breath metabolites (breath-IR model) which correlates with Homeostatic Model Assessment of Insulin Resistance (HOMA-IR) (R = 0.95, p <0.001). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We have identified a signature of 10 breath metabolites (breath-IR model), which correlates with Homeostatic Model Assessment for Insulin Resistance (HOMA-IR) (R�=�0.95, p�<�1��0.001). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our breath-IR model differentiates between adolescents with and without IR with an AUC-ROC curve of 0.87, after cross-validation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Identification of a breath signature indicative of IR shows utility of exhaled breath metabolomics for assessing systemic metabolic dysregulation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A simple and non-invasive breath-based test has potential as a diagnostic tool for monitoring IR progression. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The fatty acid and carene-enriched module has a significant positive association with preterm birth. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A total of 38 metabolites demonstrated significant changes uniquely associated with preterm birth, with 17 identified as preterm biomarkers. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of random forest achieved a highly accurate and specific prediction for preterm birth with an AUC of 0.92. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Remission classification using a combination of gray matter volume and functional connectivity led to good performing models with average 0.82-0.83 area under the curve (AUC). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Remission classification using a combination of gray matter volume and functional connectivity led to good performing models with average 0.82-0.83 area under the curve (AUC). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The models remained acceptable when validated using leave-one-site-out cross-validation with AUC values of 0.70-0.73. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive algorithm can be scaled up and applied in settings where self-reported symptoms cannot be collected. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Public health initiatives and treatment strategies targeting modifiable cardiometabolic risk factors may also improve risk trajectories and delay brain ageing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Decision support models that incorporate symptoms and available test results can assist providers in diagnosing SARS-CoV-2 infection in real-world settings. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Decision support models that incorporate symptoms and available test results can assist providers in diagnosing SARS-CoV-2 infection in real-world settings. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This claim is reproduced directly in the publication with the same framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new human mobility flow-augmented stochastic SEIR-style epidemic modeling framework can distinguish different regions and their corresponding behavior. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed modeling framework can distinguish different regions and their corresponding behaviors in the context of COVID-19 transmission. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution of the modeling framework with equivalent wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In Dane County, the most important heterogeneity affecting the spread of COVID-19 is spatial, while in Milwaukee County, ethnic heterogeneity is more apparent. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In Dane County, the most important heterogeneity affecting the spread of COVID-19 is age structure, while in Milwaukee County, racial and ethnic heterogeneity is more apparent. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication alters the specific identified heterogeneity in Dane County from 'spatial' to 'age structure', representing a substantive revision of that result while keeping the overall comparative claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The spread rate of COVID-19 shows a strong response to various reopening policies. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The spread rate of COVID-19 shows a strong response to various reopening policies, indicating the need for careful consideration of heterogeneities by policy makers. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds explicit policy implications to the observed response to reopening, clarifying the result and broadening its scope to include recommendations for policymakers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Policymakers need to carefully consider heterogeneities in communities when designing policies for mitigating the spread of COVID-19 and reopening. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The spread rate of COVID-19 shows a strong response to various reopening policies, indicating the need for careful consideration of heterogeneities by policy makers. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same policy recommendation tied to reopening effects, with equivalent emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Three different clusters among MCI subjects were discovered through consensus clustering. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Three different clusters among MCI subjects were discovered through consensus clustering. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is reproduced verbatim in the publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The discovered clusters are associated with low-risk, medium-risk, and high-risk of converting from MCI to AD. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The discovered clusters are associated with low-risk, medium-risk, and high-risk of converting from MCI to AD. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The risk associations of the clusters are stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two of the discovered classes may represent two different prodromal presentations of Alzheimer’s disease. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two of the discovered classes may represent two different prodromal presentations of Alzheimer's disease. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The interpretation about prodromal presentations is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Personalized recommendations for prescribing ACEIs and ARBs can improve patient outcomes by 1.0% compared to the standard of care. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Personalized recommendations for prescribing ACEIs and ARBs can improve patient outcomes by 1.0% compared to the standard of care. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The outcome improvement claim is identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm suggests increasing ACEI/ARBs prescriptions for patients with cardiovascular disease and decreasing prescriptions for those with low oxygen saturation at admission. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm suggests increasing ACEI/ARBs prescriptions for patients with cardiovascular disease and decreasing prescriptions for those with low oxygen saturation at admission. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The algorithmic prescribing suggestions are carried forward without change in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work offers the first personalized recommendation system to accurately evaluate the efficacy and risks of prescribing ACEIs and ARBs to hypertensive COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work offers the first personalized recommendation system to accurately evaluate the efficacy and risks of prescribing ACEIs and ARBs to hypertensive COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The claimed novelty and contribution are preserved in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study leverages an international cohort of more than 3,500 patients to identify clinical and demographic characteristics that may affect the effectiveness of ACEIs/ARBs for COVID-19 patients. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not mention the international cohort size or dataset description; this specific dataset claim is absent from the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The major changes in the percentages of different opinion groups roughly correspond to the major pandemic-related events.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim describes temporal correspondence of opinion-group percentage changes with pandemic events; this preprint claim is not represented in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The percentage of the pro-vaccine group is lower in the Southeast part of the United States.
alignments.rationale: The publication contains no regional (Southeast US) statement about lower pro-vaccine percentage, so the specific geographic claim was not retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Socioeconomically disadvantaged groups are more likely to hold polarized opinions on potential COVID-19 vaccines.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Socioeconomically disadvantaged groups were more likely to hold polarized opinions on COVID-19 vaccines, either pro-vaccine or anti-vaccine.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result (polarized opinions in socioeconomically disadvantaged groups) with equivalent meaning and similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The anti-vaccine opinion is the strongest among the people who have the worst personal pandemic experience.
alignments.pub_claim_text: People who have the worst personal pandemic experience were more likely to hold the anti-vaccine opinion.
alignments.rationale: The published claim expresses the same association between worse personal pandemic experience and anti-vaccine opinion with comparable wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Improving personal pandemic experience increases the vaccine acceptance level.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Improving personal pandemic experience increases the vaccine acceptance level.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion verbatim, with matching scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pre-transplant data led to a prediction achieving a Pearson’s correlation coefficient of r=0.39 between measured and predicted eGFR-1y.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pre-transplant data led to a prediction achieving a Pearson's correlation coefficient of r=0.38 between measured and predicted eGFR-1y.
alignments.rationale: The published result corresponds to the same finding but with a slightly updated numeric value (0.38 vs 0.39), a minor clarification rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two weeks post-transplant, the correlation was improved to r=0.63, and at the third month, to r=0.76.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two weeks post-transplant, the correlation was improved to r=0.63, and at the third month, to r=0.76.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same time-point correlations with identical values and framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cytokine stem cell factor demonstrated a strong negative correlation with eGFR.
alignments.rationale: No matching publication claim about stem cell factor (cytokine) correlation with eGFR appears in the published claims; this specific biomarker result was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The expression of the genes TMEM176B and HMMR was identified as potential prognostic markers for changes in the eGFR.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The expression of the genes TMEM176B and HMMR was identified as potential prognostic markers for changes in the eGFR after the second post-transplantation week.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same marker identification but adds a timing qualification ('after the second post-transplantation week'), narrowing the claim's scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further studies employing additional marker panels are needed to establish predictors of eGFR-1y for clinical application.
alignments.pub_claim_text: While an acceptable predictive capacity was achieved, we are still far from predicting changes in the eGFR precisely.
alignments.rationale: Both describe limitations and need for further work; the published wording emphasizes greater remaining uncertainty (tempered confidence) and reframes the limitation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ensemble of SVM-radial and PAM predicted test samples with a precision of 81.33%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ensemble learning using machine learning models resulted in a significantly higher precision of 80.41% compared to individual models.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the ensemble result with a slightly different precision value (80.41% vs 81.33%) and emphasizes significance; this is a minor numerical clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The precision of the ensemble model was significantly higher than SVM-radial and PAM.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ensemble learning using machine learning models resulted in a significantly higher precision of 80.41% compared to individual models.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates that the ensemble significantly outperformed individual models, matching the preprint claim about comparative performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ensemble model developed using microarray data classified the RNA-Sequencing samples with moderately high precision of 72.00%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RNA sequencing samples from the case-control study resulted in a moderate precision of 59.92%.
alignments.rationale: The published precision for RNA-Seq classification is substantially lower (59.92% vs 72.00%), representing a revision and reduced performance/confidence for that result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The pipeline developed in this study allows the prediction of a single microarray and RNA-Sequencing sample.
alignments.rationale: No published claim explicitly affirms single-sample prediction capability; the specific single-sample claim is not present in the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study suggested ensemble learning for higher diagnostic precision in psychiatric disorders.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study developed robust models for higher diagnostic precision in psychiatric disorders.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a stronger, more definitive statement (developed robust models) versus the preprint's suggestion, indicating an amplification of confidence and claim strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Carriers of pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants for HCM had lower left ventricular mass, but greater basal septal hypertrophy, with reduced lifespan compared to genotype negative individuals.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Carriers of pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants for HCM had lower left ventricular mass, but greater basal septal hypertrophy, with reduced life span compared with genotype negative individuals.
alignments.rationale: The published claim is effectively identical in content and certainty to the preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Four main phenotypic branches of HCM were identified using unsupervised learning of three-dimensional shape.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Four main phenotypic branches of HCM were identified using unsupervised learning of 3-dimensional shape.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same unsupervised-learning-derived phenotypic branching result with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Polygenic risk for HCM was associated with different patterns and degrees of disease expression.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Polygenic risk for HCM was associated with different patterns and degrees of disease expression.
alignments.rationale: The published claim matches the preprint in substance and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The data-driven taxonomy of HCM will be of value in understanding the causes and consequences of disease diversity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The data-driven taxonomy of HCM will be of value in understanding the causes and consequences of disease diversity.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion about the value of the data-driven taxonomy without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel blood-based glycoproteomic platform identified glycopeptide biomarkers that differentiate between patients with benign pelvic masses and malignant epithelial ovarian cancer (EOC).
alignments.pub_claim_text: A biomarker panel was identified that distinguished benign lesions from epithelial ovarian cancer (EOC) with sensitivity and specificity of 83.5% and 90.1% in the training set, and 86.7% in both sensitivity and specificity in the test set.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same identification of a blood-based biomarker panel distinguishing benign from EOC, matching the preprint's core result and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classifier generated from a subset of these markers discriminated between benign pelvic tumors and EOC with sensitivity and specificity of 83.5% and 90.1% in the training set and 86.7% in the testing set, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A biomarker panel was identified that distinguished benign lesions from epithelial ovarian cancer (EOC) with sensitivity and specificity of 83.5% and 90.1% in the training set, and 86.7% in both sensitivity and specificity in the test set.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides the same classifier performance metrics for training and test sets, matching the preprint's reported sensitivities and specificities.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients with late-stage EOC (FIGO stage III and IV) had markedly higher levels of tri- and tetra-antennary glycopeptide markers containing fucose.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fucosylated multi-antennary glycopeptide markers were found to be higher in late-stage than in early-stage EOC.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same stage-associated increase in fucosylated multi-antennary glycopeptide markers, matching the preprint finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides mechanistic insights suggesting a direct link between the tumor site and the circulating glycoproteome.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study supports the hypothesis that blood glycoproteomic profiling can be used for EOC diagnosis and staging.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the finding as support for the diagnostic/staging utility of profiling rather than explicitly describing mechanistic linkage; the claim is therefore clarified/shifted in emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generated blood glycoproteomic profiles may inform the development of robust clinical tests to diagnose and stage patients with EOC.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Blood glycopeptide biomarkers have the potential to distinguish benign from malignant pelvic masses and early- from late-stage EOC.
alignments.rationale: Both claims express the potential of blood glycoproteomic profiles/biomarkers to inform diagnostic and staging tests, with aligned scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FastText exhibits the highest similarity with theoretical clustering patterns obtained from biomedical terminologies, with a similarity score of 0.88, 0.80, and 0.92 for diagnosis, procedure, and medication codes, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FastText exhibits the highest similarity with theoretical clustering patterns obtained from biomedical terminologies, with a similarity score of 0.88, 0.80, and 0.92 for diagnosis, procedure, and medication codes, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numeric similarity results and wording, indicating no substantive change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Word2vec and GloVe tend to outperform fastText in outcome prediction, with word2vec achieving AUROC scores of 0.78, 0.62, and 0.85 for length-of-stay, readmission, and mortality prediction, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Word2vec and GloVe tend to outperform fastText in outcome prediction, with word2vec achieving AUROC scores of 0.78, 0.62, and 0.85 for length-of-stay, readmission, and mortality prediction, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same comparative outcome-prediction results and identical AUROC values, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GloVe achieves the highest performance for diagnosis and medication codes in predicting medical codes in patient trajectories, with AUPRC scores of 0.45 and 0.81, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GloVe achieves the highest performance for diagnosis and medication codes at the highest level of the semantic hierarchy, with AUPRC scores of 0.45 and 0.81, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same AUPRC results but specifies that the performance is at the highest semantic-hierarchy level, narrowing/clarifying the original claim about patient-trajectory code prediction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Subword information is crucial for learning medical concept representations, but global embedding vectors are better suited for high-level downstream tasks, such as trajectory prediction. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Subword information is crucial for learning medical concept representations, but global embedding vectors are better suited for high-level downstream tasks, such as trajectory prediction. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conceptual conclusion about subword vs global embeddings with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study highlights the potential of using machine learning techniques to semantically encode medical data. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study highlights the potential of using machine learning techniques to semantically encode medical data. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both versions state the same contribution about the potential of ML to semantically encode medical data with identical certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Radiation Oncology Learning Health System (RO-LHS) improves the quality of care by integrating clinical, dosimetry, treatment delivery, and research data in real-time. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tools enhance the quality of care by enabling the identification of cohorts, clinical decision support, and the development of clinical studies and machine learning programs in radiation oncology. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The published text reframes the preprint's strong real-time integration claim into a more operational description of tool-enabled care improvements, reducing the specificity about real-time integration and softening the causal language.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The knowledge graph-based approach ensures that radiotherapy data is easily discoverable and accessible for clinical decision support systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tools enhance the quality of care by enabling the identification of cohorts, clinical decision support, and the development of clinical studies and machine learning programs in radiation oncology. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the preprint's claim by explicitly linking discoverability/accessibility to downstream uses (cohort identification and clinical decision support), clarifying the intended applications without changing certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patient similarity analysis using embedding models indicated that the Word2Vec model had the highest mean cosine similarity. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patient similarity analysis using embedding models showed that the Word2Vec model had the highest mean cosine similarity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about Word2Vec having the highest mean cosine similarity is preserved verbatim, with the publication presenting it with stronger certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework and tools contribute to continuous learning and improvement in patient care by facilitating data discovery and analysis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework and tools described support the development of a Radiation Oncology Learning Health System (RO-LHS). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The published claim reframes the contribution as supporting RO-LHS development rather than directly asserting continuous learning and care improvement, clarifying and slightly narrowing the original framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tools enhance the quality of care by enabling the identification of cohorts and supporting the development of clinical studies and machine learning programs in radiation oncology. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tools enhance the quality of care by enabling the identification of cohorts, clinical decision support, and the development of clinical studies and machine learning programs in radiation oncology. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same substantive claim, adding 'clinical decision support' explicitly but preserving the original intent and certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The data pipeline and tool were tested with 1660 patient clinical and dosimetry records resulting in 504,180 RDF tuples and visualized data relationships using graph-based representations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This factual testing/result about dataset size and RDF tuples appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint, so it is an added empirical claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The precision well-being approach categorizes medical students into distinct groups based on their holistic mental health. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This novel precision well-being framework represents an integrated empirical model that classifies individual medical students into distinct and meaningful well-being phenotypes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication rephrases the contribution in more formal terms (an integrated empirical model), clarifying the approach while preserving the core claim of categorizing students into phenotypes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Analysis identified three distinct clusters of medical students: 'Healthy Flourishers', 'Getting By', and 'At-Risk', each with unique mental health patterns. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis identified three distinct clusters of medical students based on their mental health metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions report the identification of three distinct clusters; the publication condenses wording but preserves the substantive finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Students in the 'Healthy Flourishers' cluster reported no signs of anxiety or depression and high levels of flourishing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Membership in the 'Healthy Flourishers' well-being phenotype was associated with no signs of anxiety or depression while simultaneously reporting high levels of flourishing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same association between the 'Healthy Flourishers' phenotype and low anxiety/depression and high flourishing with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can be used to monitor students' transitions between clusters and evaluate the effectiveness of personalized intervention strategies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach has direct applicability to student support and can be used to evaluate the effectiveness of personalized intervention strategies stratified by cluster membership. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly links the model to student support and stratified evaluation of interventions, clarifying the practical applicability described in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Nearly half (49%) of the medical students surveyed were classified as 'Healthy Flourishers'. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces this specific prevalence statistic for a cluster that was not reported in the preprint, so it is an added empirical claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Video-based objective symptom quantification enabled by machine learning introduces a potential solution for assessing motor symptom progression in Parkinson's disease patients. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Video-based objective symptom quantification enabled by machine learning introduces a potential solution for assessing motor symptom progression in Parkinson’s disease patients. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same high-level contribution claim about a video-based ML solution for assessing PD motor symptom progression.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed framework accurately predicts high versus low Parkinson's disease motor symptom severity according to MDS-UPDRS Part III metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed interpretable video-based framework predicts high versus low Parkinson’s disease motor symptom severity according to MDS-UPDRS Part III metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the predictive result with the same target metrics and certainty, adding the descriptor 'interpretable' but not changing the claim's substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework eliminates the requirement for specialized equipment by using retrospective, single-view, seconds-long videos recorded on consumer-grade devices. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework eliminates the requirement for specialized equipment by using retrospective, single-view, seconds-long videos recorded on consumer-grade devices. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same technological claim but presents it with stronger certainty and as a contribution, amplifying confidence in the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework quantifies clinically meaningful motor features useful for both machine learning predictions and clinical analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework validated and robustly quantified canonical movement features and identified new clinical insights related to clinical severity, including pinkie finger movements and lower limb and axial features of gait. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides specific validated features and new clinical insights, expanding and clarifying the preprint's general claim about clinically meaningful features, while reporting somewhat reduced certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sodium features from 23 Na-MRI are proposed as biomarkers of neuronal excitability related to the epileptogenic zone network (EZN). (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The sodium features from <sup>23</sup>Na-MRI are proposed as biomarkers of neuronal excitability related to the epileptogenic zone network (EZN). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the biomarker proposal but reports it with higher certainty, amplifying confidence in the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of 23 Na-MRI priors in the Virtual Epileptic Patient (VEP) pipeline improved the estimation of the EZN in terms of balanced accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The statistical results demonstrated that the VEP based on <sup>23</sup>Na-MRI prior has better balanced accuracy compared to the current VEP. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same balanced-accuracy improvement for the VEP using 23Na-MRI priors, preserving the result and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 23 Na-MRI priors inferred EZN estimates that were significantly closer to clinical hypotheses than previously used priors or the no-prior condition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the claimed 'closer to clinical hypotheses' finding; instead it reports related but different metrics, so this specific claim appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mapping from 23 Na-MRI features to predict the EZN was achieved using a machine learning approach. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mapping from <sup>23</sup>Na-MRI features to predict the EZN was successfully proposed using a machine learning approach. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the machine-learning mapping claim and presents it with stronger certainty, amplifying confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The VEP based on <sup>23</sup>Na-MRI prior achieves a similar weighted harmonic mean of precision and recall as the current VEP. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific performance comparison (weighted harmonic mean parity) is reported in the publication but was not present in the preprint, so it is an added result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identified 114, 119, and 165 potentially pleiotropic variants for cannabis use disorder (CUD) severity in American Indians, Mexican Americans, and European Americans respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identified 114, 119, and 165 potentially pleiotropic variants for cannabis use disorder (CUD) severity in American Indians, Mexican Americans, and European Americans respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The wording and numeric results are identical between preprint and publication, with no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ten pleiotropic loci were shared between the cohorts, although the exact variants from each cohort differed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ten pleiotropic loci were shared between the cohorts, although the exact variants from each cohort differed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim text and interpretation are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The pleiotropic genes were significantly up-regulated in most brain tissues but down-regulated in the cerebellum and hypothalamus. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pleiotropic genes were significantly up-regulated in most brain tissues but down-regulated in the cerebellum and hypothalamus. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The statement is the same but the publication downgrades certainty from high to medium, so content unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest that different population cohorts may have distinct genetic factors for CUD, but they share pleiotropic genes from underlying pathways related to Alzheimer's disease, neuroplasticity, immune response, and reproductive endocrine systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings suggest that different population cohorts may have distinct genetic factors for CUD, but they share pleiotropic genes from underlying pathways related to Alzheimer's disease, neuroplasticity, immune response, and reproductive endocrine systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion language and certainty are the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This study is the first to attempt a large-scale pleiotropy detection scan for CUD severity. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This contribution-level statement appears in the publication but was not present among the listed preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individual mobility metrics varied significantly by counties and by calendar time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This descriptive result about spatial and temporal variability does not appear among the publication claims and therefore is treated as removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: More than 50% of the variability in the data was explained by the first principal component by each state, indicating good dimension reduction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: More than 50% of the variability in the data was explained by the first principal component by each state, indicating good dimension reduction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The PCA result is reported verbatim in the publication with unchanged interpretation and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our mobility index was independently associated with COVID-19 cases in all four states given varying time-lags. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our multidimensional mobility index (MI) was independently associated with COVID-19 cases in all four states given varying time-lags. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core result that the multidimensional mobility index was independently associated with cases is preserved and equally certain in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A single metric of mobility was not sensitive enough to capture the complexity of human interactions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An individual metric of mobility was not associated with surges of COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the idea more specifically as a lack of association with surges (a narrower, empirically stronger statement) and reports it with higher certainty than the preprint's more general, moderate-confidence conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Monitoring mobility can be an important public health tool, however, it should be modelled as a multidimensional construct. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Monitoring mobility can be an important public health tool, however, it should be modelled as a multidimensional construct. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The recommendation to model mobility as multidimensional is carried forward unchanged in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Minimizing human interactions remains an effective strategy to mitigate the spread of SARS-CoV-2. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This high-level public-health conclusion appears in the published paper but was not listed among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identifies clear signatures of immunologic activity between PASC patients and those with uncomplicated recovery, dominated by inflammatory cytokine signaling, neutrophil activity, and markers of cell death. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients with PASC exhibit ongoing antiviral B cell development, immune-mediated fibrosis, and markers of cell death, which are not present in controls with uncomplicated recovery. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements report immune differences (including markers of cell death) between PASC and controls; the publication emphasizes specific processes (B cell development, fibrosis) that clarify the preprint's broader immunologic signature.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Distinct PASC subsets, termed plasma quiescent (plaq) and inflammatory (infl) PASC, reveal heterogeneity in ongoing inflammatory processes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PASC can be stratified into inflammatory and non-inflammatory types based on plasma and immune cell profiling. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The stratification into inflammatory and non-inflammatory subtypes is the same concept, with consistent terminology and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: InflPASC patients display positive clinical tests of acute inflammation, including elevated levels of C-reactive protein and fibrinogen, along with increased B cell activity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inflammatory PASC is identifiable through a refined set of 12 blood markers and shows evidence of ongoing neutrophil activity, B cell memory alterations, and building autoreactivity more than a year post COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines and expands the preprint's marker-based description (providing a 12-marker set and additional longitudinal evidence), broadening the specific evidence base while retaining the core finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The unique plasma signatures of PASC patients enable the creation of refined models with high sensitivity and specificity for identifying inflPASC. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inflammatory PASC is identifiable through a refined set of 12 blood markers and shows evidence of ongoing neutrophil activity, B cell memory alterations, and building autoreactivity more than a year post COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both emphasize identification of inflammatory PASC via a refined marker set; the publication specifies the 12-marker panel, clarifying the preprint's description of refined models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Refined ML modeling highlights the significance of several markers, including the peptide hormone epiregulin, for potential diagnostic or therapeutic use in PASC. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Specific disease features in post-acute sequelae of COVID-19 (PASC) associate with discrete immune processes and therapeutic opportunities. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same overarching conclusion about therapeutic opportunities linked to immune features; the preprint's specific emphasis on epiregulin is less prominent in the publication, so the claim is treated as a clarified, slightly more general conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The highest accuracy in distinguishing between RA patients who improved and did not improve their levels of fatigue was achieved with the structural MRI set at 67.9%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The highest accuracy in distinguishing between RA patients who improved and did not improve their levels of fatigue was achieved with the structural MRI set at 67.9%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Classification performance using clinical features was at the chance level. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Classification performance using clinical features was at the chance level, indicating their ineffectiveness in predicting fatigue improvement. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands slightly on the implication (ineffectiveness) but does not change the substantive result; classified as clarified with unchanged confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean curvature of the left superior temporal sulcus was the most strongly selected feature for predicting fatigue improvement. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean curvature of the left superior temporal sulcus was the most strongly selected feature for predicting fatigue improvement. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The feature selection result is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results presented in this study evidence a superiority of brain metrics over clinical metrics in predicting fatigue changes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results provide evidence that brain metrics are superior to clinical metrics in predicting changes in fatigue levels in RA patients. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is preserved with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further exploration of these methods may enable clinicians to triage patients towards the most appropriate fatigue alleviating therapies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Further exploration of brain metrics may assist clinicians in triaging patients towards appropriate fatigue alleviating therapies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Recommendation is consistent between preprint and publication with no substantive difference.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The score developed by Sieira et al. showed the best performance for risk stratification in the whole Brugada Syndrome cohort with an AUC of 0.805. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The score developed by Sieira et al showed the best performance for the whole cohort with an AUC of 0.806. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is effectively identical aside from trivial rounding of the AUC; interpretation and scope are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel risk score developed based on the Sieira score achieved the highest AUC of 0.855. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel risk score developed using the Sieira score and additional significant variables achieved an AUC of 0.855. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported novel score and its AUC are the same in both versions; publication clarifies inclusion of additional variables but does not change the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Gaussian Naïve Bayes model demonstrated the best performance with an AUC of 0.97 compared to logistic regression and nearest neighbor models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A gradient boosting classifier model demonstrated the best performance for the intermediate risk subgroup with an AUC of 0.814. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The model performance claim changes substantially between preprint and publication (different best model and different AUC reported for a subgroup), indicating a substantive revision and narrowing of the claim to a specific subgroup.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inclusion of investigation results and more complex models are needed to improve the predictive performance of risk scores in the intermediate risk Brugada Syndrome population. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A simpler risk score based on non-invasive results showed a statistically comparable AUC of 0.784, which improved to 0.942 using random survival forests. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides concrete evidence that more complex models (random survival forests) can substantially improve AUC, turning a recommendation into a stronger, higher-certainty result; this clarifies and strengthens the preprint's suggestion.
