alignments.rationale: The clinical validation trend is restated without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model incorrectly assessed three cases well above the clinical threshold of 10%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model incorrectly assessed 3 cases well above the clinical threshold of 10%, indicating limitations in its accuracy. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds interpretation that these misassessments indicate accuracy limitations, clarifying the implication of the error cases.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integrated gradient suggested potential sources of false positives including rouleaux formations, cell boundaries, and precipitate. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integrated gradient suggested potential sources of false positives including rouleaux formations, cell boundaries, and precipitate. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The explanation of likely false-positive sources is preserved exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This project highlights the need for integrated testing of ML-based models, even when models in the development phase perform well. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This project highlights the need for integrated testing of machine learning-based models, even when models in the development phase perform well. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding recommendation about integrated testing of ML models is unchanged in meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Plasma quinolinic acid (QA) is associated with increases in LBG glutamate (Glu), while kynurenic acid (KYNA) and KYNA/QA are associated with decreases in LBG Glu. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Plasma kynurenic acid (KYNA) and KYNA/QA were associated with decreases and quinolinic acid (QA) with increases in left basal ganglia Glu. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same associations (QA increases; KYNA and KYNA/QA decreases in LBG Glu) with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Plasma kynurenine/tryptophan and CSF 3-hydroxy kynurenine (3HK) are associated with increases in LBG myo-inositol (mI). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Plasma kynurenine/tryptophan (KYN/TRP) ratio and cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) 3-hydroxykynurenine (3HK) were associated with increases in left basal ganglia myo-inositol. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same two biomarkers associated with increased LBG myo-inositol, matching the preprint claim closely.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Plasma and CSF kynurenine pathway metabolites are associated with decreases in regional homogeneity (ReHo) in the left basal ganglia (LBG) and dorsomedial prefrontal cortex (DMPFC), as well as impaired functional connectivity between these regions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Plasma and CSF KP were associated with decreases in ReHo in the basal ganglia and dorsomedial prefrontal regions (DMPFC) and impaired functional connectivity between these two regions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same associations (decreased ReHo in BG and DMPFC and impaired connectivity) with the same anatomical specificity and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DMPFC-BG connectivity mediates the effect of plasma and CSF kynurenine pathway metabolites on anhedonia. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DMPFC-basal ganglia mediated the effect of plasma and CSF KP on anhedonia. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The mediation claim is retained in the publication but reported with higher certainty (publication labels it high), so the claim content is unchanged but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings highlight the contribution of kynurenine pathway metabolites to glial and neuronal dysfunction and ultimately behavior in depression. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings highlight the pathological impact of KP system dysregulation in mediating inflammatory behaviors such as anhedonia. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows and reframes the broad neuroglial dysfunction/depression claim to KP dysregulation specifically mediating inflammatory behaviors (e.g., anhedonia) and reports lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Weakened connectivity between the Salience Network and Default Mode Network core regions upon acute stress induction at baseline predicted longitudinal increases in perceived stress level. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Weakened connectivity between the salience network and default mode network core regions upon acute-stress induction at baseline predicted longitudinal increases in perceived-stress level. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same predictive result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Increased coupling between the overall Salience Network and anterior cerebellum was observed in participants with higher clinician-rated PTSD symptoms, particularly intrusion levels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increased coupling between the overall salience network and anterior cerebellum was observed in participants with higher clinician-rated PTSD symptoms, particularly intrusion levels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This result is reproduced verbatim in the publication with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reconfiguration of large-scale neural networks upon acute stress induction is relevant for assessing and detecting risk and resilience factors for PTSD. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reconfiguration of large-scale neural networks upon acute-stress induction is relevant for assessing and detecting risk and resilience factors for PTSD. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about network reconfiguration and its relevance for PTSD risk/resilience.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SN connectivity changes may serve as a potential marker for trauma-related symptom development, sensitive even in a relatively resilient sample. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Changes in the connectivity strength of large-scale neurocognitive networks upon acute stress may predict vulnerability to long-term stress effects. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the preprint's specific SN marker claim to large-scale network connectivity predicting vulnerability and reports the conclusion with higher certainty, broadening the scope and amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate a development of a clinical specific BERT model with a huge size of Japanese clinical narrative. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate the development of a clinical specific BERT model with a huge amount of Japanese clinical text. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the contribution claim about developing a clinical BERT on a large Japanese clinical corpus with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracies of Masked LM and Next Sentence Prediction were 0.773 and 0.975, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracies of Masked-LM and Next Sentence Prediction were 0.773 and 0.975, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical evaluation results are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed BERT tends to show higher performances on the MedWeb task than the other nonspecific BERTs. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed BERT did not show significantly higher performance on the MedWeb task than the other BERT models that were pre-trained with Japanese Wikipedia text. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication contradicts the preprint's suggestion of higher MedWeb performance and instead reports no significant superiority, effectively revising and reducing support for the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: No significant differences were found between the developed BERT and other nonspecific BERTs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed BERT did not show significantly higher performance on the MedWeb task than the other BERT models that were pre-trained with Japanese Wikipedia text. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the null result about MedWeb performance equivalence with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The advantage of training on domain-specific texts may become apparent in the more complex tasks on actual clinical text. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The advantage of pre-training on clinical text may become apparent in more complex tasks on actual clinical text. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same conditional conclusion but expresses it more clearly and with higher reported certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Visceral adipose tissue volume, muscle adipose tissue infiltration, and liver fat fraction are the strongest predictors of increased cardiovascular age-delta for both sexes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Visceral adipose tissue volume, muscle adipose tissue infiltration, and liver fat fraction are the strongest predictors of increased cardiovascular age-delta for both sexes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same set of strongest predictors and same sex-general conclusion with identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Abdominal subcutaneous adipose tissue volume is associated with increased age-delta only in males. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Abdominal subcutaneous adipose tissue volume and android fat mass are associated with increased age-delta only in males. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The association between abdominal subcutaneous adipose tissue and increased age-delta in males is preserved in the publication; the publication additionally reports android fat mass but the original claim remains intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Genetically-predicted gluteo-femoral fat shows an association with decreased age-delta. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Genetically predicted gynoid fat is associated with decreased age-delta. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: 'Gluteo-femoral' corresponds to 'gynoid' fat terminology; the association with decreased age-delta is preserved with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work demonstrates the contribution of sex-dependent patterns of visceral adipose tissue and subcutaneous fat distribution to biological cardiovascular ageing in middle aged adults. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Shared and sex-specific patterns of body fat are associated with both protective and harmful changes in cardiovascular ageing. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication rephrases the conclusion to emphasize both shared and sex-specific patterns and protective/harmful associations, clarifying but not changing the overall conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings highlight the potential for strategies to attenuate ageing through modifying adipose tissue function. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adipose tissue distribution and function are key targets for interventions to extend healthy lifespan. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames adipose tissue as key intervention targets (stronger phrasing) compared with the preprint's phrasing about potential strategies, making the conclusion more directive.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nationality, sport practice, and eyeglasses usage are significant PEL confounding factors influencing the eye microbiome. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nationality, allergy, sport practice, and eyeglasses usage are significant PEL confounding factors influencing the eye microbiome. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the original factors but adds 'allergy' as an additional significant factor, broadening the set of reported confounders while keeping certainty high.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Alpha-diversity analysis showed higher microbial richness in Spanish subjects compared to Italian subjects and higher biodiversity in sports practitioners. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Alpha-diversity analysis revealed significant differences between Spanish and Italian subjects, with a median Shannon index of 1.05 for Spanish subjects and 0.59 for Italian subjects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides numeric detail for the same alpha-diversity differences reported in the preprint, clarifying the result without changing its scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Beta-diversity analysis indicated significant differences in microbial community composition based on nationality, age, sport, and eyeglasses usage. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the preprint's explicit beta-diversity claim or the listed covariates, so this specific result was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Differential abundance analysis identified several microbial genera associated with these PEL factors. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No differential-abundance results identifying specific genera are presented in the publication, indicating removal of this claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study underscores the importance of considering PEL factors when studying the ocular microbiome. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study underscores the importance of considering PEL factors when studying the ocular microbiome. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the overall conclusion but reports it with slightly lower stated certainty (medium), so the claim is unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Support Vector Machine (SVM) model for Nationality achieved an accuracy of 100%, with an AUC-ROC score of 1.0, indicating excellent performance in classifying microbial profiles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The SVM performance metrics appear as a new quantitative result in the publication that was not reported in the preprint, so this is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Whole-genome sequencing (WGS) is more accurate than targeted sequencing assays for detecting copy number variants (CNVs). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim explicitly compares WGS to targeted sequencing; the published claims report tool performance and implications but do not restate a direct WGS-vs-targeted conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different CNV detection tools exhibit strengths in either sensitivity or precision, but few provide the balanced performance necessary for clinical testing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Whole-genome sequencing (WGS) CNV detection tools vary in sensitivity from 7% to 83% and in precision from 1% to 76%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication quantifies the variability in sensitivity/precision, clarifying the preprint's general statement about differing strengths across tools without changing its scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DRAGEN™ v4.2 CNV caller with custom filters on its high sensitivity mode offers superior performance compared to other CNV detection tools. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DRAGEN v4.2 high-sensitivity CNV calls, after post-processing with custom filters, achieved 100% sensitivity and 77% precision on the optimized gene panel. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports specific performance metrics for DRAGEN v4.2 on an optimized gene panel, providing quantitative clarification while narrowing the claim to the evaluated panel rather than a broad superiority claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Callers generally demonstrate better performance for deletions than for duplications, especially for events shorter than 5kb. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Callers generally perform better for deletions, achieving up to 88% sensitivity, compared to duplications, which have a maximum sensitivity of 47%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the overall deletion>duplication conclusion and quantifies sensitivity but omits the preprint's explicit size-specific caveat (<5kb), thus revising and narrowing the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of genomic sequencing and qPCR-based protocols enhances the monitoring of variant Omicron sublineages. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This methodology enhances molecular surveillance strategies and supports global efforts in pandemic control. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication attributes improved surveillance capacity to the developed methodology, matching the preprint's claim about enhancing monitoring of sublineages.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Support Vector Machine (SVM) model achieved approximately 96.52% accuracy in lineage assignment compared to the test dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The support vector machine (SVM) model demonstrates ∼96.52% accuracy in lineage assignment after fine-tuning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim reproduces the same accuracy result for the SVM model, with no meaningful change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed qPCR assay can effectively distinguish between 'BE.9' and 'non-BE.9' lineages. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed qPCR-based assay achieves 93.3% sensitivity, 95.1% specificity, and 92.4% agreement when validated against lineage predictions from next-generation sequencing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication validates the assay with performance metrics against sequencing-based lineage calls, clarifying assay accuracy though it frames results generally rather than explicitly naming the BE.9 distinction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods provide a cost-effective alternative to traditional sequencing methods for monitoring SARS-CoV-2 variants. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analytical method introduced is cost-effective and scalable, making it suitable for rapid assessment of emerging SARS-CoV-2 variants in resource-limited settings. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates that the method is cost-effective and suitable for variant monitoring, matching the preprint's cost-effectiveness conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Single-feature beta-band predictions demonstrated positive performance scores in 94% of the outcomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Single-feature beta-band predictions demonstrated positive performance scores in 94% of the outcomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and metrics in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predictions based on single non-beta-frequency features yielded positive scores in only 25% of the outcomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predictions based on single non-beta-frequency features yielded positive scores in only 25% of the outcomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and metrics in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multi-feature predictions achieved accuracy scores within the two highest performance classes more often than single beta-based predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multi-feature predictions achieved accuracy scores within the two highest performance classes more often than single beta-based predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predicting the optimal stimulation contact based on LFP recordings is feasible and can improve DBS programming efficiency in PD. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predicting the optimal stimulation contact based on LFP recordings is feasible and can improve DBS programming efficiency in PD. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical conclusion in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future research should further explore multi-feature predictions for optimal contact identification. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Future research should further explore multi-feature predictions for optimal contact identification. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Recommendation is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a higher prevalence and incidence of Alzheimer's Disease and Related Dementias in rural areas compared to urban areas. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A majority of studies indicate a higher prevalence and incidence of Alzheimer's disease and related dementias in rural areas. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding about higher rural prevalence/incidence, matching the preprint's result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Common risk factors for Alzheimer's Disease and Related Dementias include female gender, lower education level, lower income, and comorbidities such as diabetes and cerebrovascular diseases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Common risk factors for Alzheimer's disease and related dementias include female sex, lower education level, lower income, and comorbidities such as diabetes and cerebrovascular diseases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim lists the same risk factors with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Environmental and lifestyle factors for Alzheimer's Disease and Related Dementias have been less explored in the existing literature. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Environmental and lifestyle factors affecting Alzheimer's disease and related dementias have been less explored in the literature. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Both versions note the underexploration of environmental and lifestyle factors; the publication frames it as a limitation but preserves the substance of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The review identifies significant gaps in research regarding rural-urban disparities in Alzheimer's Disease and Related Dementias, particularly in underexplored geographic regions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The review emphasizes the need for future research to utilize advanced statistical methods, such as machine learning and causal inference, to investigate understudied factors contributing to Alzheimer's disease and related dementias disparities. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts emphasis from geographic gaps to methodological recommendations for investigating understudied factors, clarifying the form of future work while narrowing the claim's focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The statistical methods used in the studies reviewed primarily relied on traditional analyses and did not incorporate advanced techniques such as machine learning or causal inference methods. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inconsistent definitions of 'rural' complicate cross-country comparisons of Alzheimer's disease and related dementias disparities. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication highlights a different limitation (inconsistent rural definitions) not explicitly emphasized in the preprint's statement about statistical methods; this represents a revision in the limitation focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Support-vector machine (SVM) demonstrated better performance in the test dataset in distinguishing between benign and HCC nodules, achieving an accuracy of 0.92, sensitivity of 0.94, and specificity of 0.86. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Support-vector machine (SVM) demonstrated better performance in the test dataset in distinguishing between benign and HCC nodules, achieving an accuracy of 0.92, sensitivity of 0.94, and specificity of 0.86. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance metrics and conclusion are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Utilizing diffusion-MRI radiomics highlights the performance of SVM, trained on lesions’ diffusivity characteristics, in distinguishing benign and HCC nodules. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Utilizing diffusion-MRI radiomics highlights the performance of SVM, trained on lesions' diffusivity characteristics, in distinguishing benign and HCC nodules. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion but reduces the stated certainty (high→medium), indicating a tempered confidence in the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integration of diffusion radiomics with machine learning models demonstrates potential for achieving a reliable noninvasive method to improve the current diagnosis criteria. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integration of diffusion radiomics for monitoring water restriction patterns as tumour histopathological index demonstrates potential for achieving a reliable noninvasive method to improve the current diagnostic criteria. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that diffusion radiomics monitors water restriction patterns as a histopathological index, clarifying and narrowing the preprint's more general statement about integration with ML for diagnosis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further evaluations be conducted on multi-center datasets to address harmonization challenges. (certainty=low, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Further evaluations be conducted on multicentre datasets to address harmonization challenges. (certainty=low, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The recommendation to evaluate multicentre datasets to address harmonization is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Random Forest and XGBoost models showed superior predictive performance for post-operative atrial fibrillation, with test sensitivities of 0.76 and 0.83, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Random Forest and XGBoost models demonstrated superior predictive performance for POAF, with test area under the curve (AUC) values of 0.76 and 0.83, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The models and numeric values match, but the metric changed from 'sensitivities' in the preprint to 'AUC' in the publication, so the claim is revised rather than unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Four upregulated miRNAs—hsa-miR-96-5p, hsa-miR-184, hsa-miR-17-3p, and hsa-miR-200-3p—were identified as part of the AFib-miRNA signature.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Four upregulated miRNAs—hsa-miR-96-5p, hsa-miR-184, hsa-miR-17-3p, and hsa-miR-200-3p—overlapped with the POAF-miRNA signature and were significantly associated with various cardiovascular diseases.
alignments.rationale: The same four miRNAs are reported, and the publication adds that they overlapped with a POAF signature and disease associations, which clarifies and expands context without changing the core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AFib-miRNA signature was significantly associated with various cardiovascular diseases, including acute myocardial infarction, hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, and heart failure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Four upregulated miRNAs—hsa-miR-96-5p, hsa-miR-184, hsa-miR-17-3p, and hsa-miR-200-3p—overlapped with the POAF-miRNA signature and were significantly associated with various cardiovascular diseases.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same association between the miRNA signature and cardiovascular diseases using similar language and disease examples.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identified miRNAs demonstrate significant potential as predictive biomarkers for atrial fibrillation post-coronary artery bypass grafting.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pre-operative, circulating microRNAs (miRNAs) can serve as potential biomarkers for post-operative atrial fibrillation (POAF) in patients undergoing coronary artery bypass grafting (CABG).
alignments.rationale: Both claims state the same conclusion that pre-operative circulating miRNAs can act as potential biomarkers for POAF following CABG.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings suggest that miRNA signatures could enhance predictive accuracy for atrial fibrillation, offering a novel, noninvasive approach to early detection and personalized management of this condition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: miRNA signatures could enhance predictive accuracy for POAF, offering a novel, noninvasive approach to early detection and personalized management of this condition.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about miRNA signatures improving predictive accuracy and providing a noninvasive approach for POAF management.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The identified miRNAs target key signaling pathways involved in cardiovascular pathology, such as the MAPK, PI3K-Akt, and TGF-beta signaling pathways.
alignments.rationale: This mechanistic pathway-targeting claim appears in the publication but was not present among the preprint claims, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Serum miRNAs can predict the biological impact of radiation doses in humans treated with total body irradiation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Serum miRNAs can be used to predict the biological impact of radiation doses in humans treated with total body irradiation.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion about serum miRNAs predicting biological impact of TBI in humans with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An 8-miRNA-based model demonstrated an AUC of 0.97 and accuracy greater than 91% for detecting radiation exposure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A model based on the expression of specific miRNAs achieved an AUC of 0.9 for identifying samples drawn after irradiation.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a lower AUC (0.9 vs 0.97) and omits the reported >91% accuracy, indicating the model performance claim was revised and tempered in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MiRNAs with detectable expression can separate irradiated from non-irradiated samples across species, validating their role as radiation-responsive biomarkers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Differential expression of miRNAs allows for the distinction between irradiated and non-irradiated samples in mice, macaques, and humans.
alignments.rationale: Both versions report cross-species separation of irradiated vs non-irradiated samples using miRNA expression, matching in content and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Serum miRNAs may serve as functional biodosimeters for identifying individuals exposed to clinically significant radiation doses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Serum miRNAs reflect radiation exposure and dose for humans undergoing total body irradiation.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the claim as miRNAs reflecting exposure and dose, which clarifies and operationalizes the preprint's 'functional biodosimeter' concept without changing the core meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Frustration is characterized as an evolving brain network process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two modules present in the baseline pretask resting-state scan contributed most to brain circuit reorganization during and after frustration.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides specific evidence of network reorganization (modules contributing to reorganization), which clarifies the preprint's broader conceptual claim that frustration is an evolving network process.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The post-frustration recovery period is important for the pathophysiology of irritability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These pilot results suggest the importance of the postfrustration recovery period in the pathophysiology of irritability.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states the same conclusion about the importance of the postfrustration recovery period for irritability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: E glob of brain networks that emerged after the frustrating task predicted self- and observer-rated irritability in previously unseen data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Only E<sub>glob</sub> of modules in the posttask resting-state scans predicted irritability symptoms.
alignments.rationale: Both claims report that Eglob in posttask modules predicted irritability symptoms; the publication frames it more generally but preserves the central result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Self-reported irritability was predicted by E glob of a fronto-temporal-limbic module.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Self-reported irritability was predicted by E<sub>glob</sub> of a frontotemporal-limbic module.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this specific module-level prediction for self-reported irritability verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Observer-rated irritability was predicted by E glob of motor-parietal and ventral-prefrontal-subcortical modules.
alignments.pub_claim_text: E<sub>glob</sub> in 3 specific posttask modules predicted irritability and could represent specific intervention targets for irritability.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the result that specific posttask modules' Eglob predicted irritability but reports it more generally (three modules and potential intervention targets) rather than naming the observer-rated modules explicitly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a positive correlation between COVID-19-related mental health concerns on Twitter and the severity of the COVID-19 pandemic in the UK.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a positive correlation between mental health concerns on Twitter and the COVID-19 pandemic in the US.
alignments.rationale: The association between pandemic severity and Twitter mental health concerns is preserved, but the geographic context changed from the UK to the US in the publication, so the claim is revised and narrowed geographically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Populated urban areas have a higher proportion of Twitter users with mental health concerns compared to England as a whole.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report this England-versus-urban finding; instead it reports different geographic patterns (US east/west coasts), so this preprint claim appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Middle-aged and older adults are more likely to suffer from mental health issues or express their mental health concerns on Twitter during the COVID-19 pandemic.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Males, White individuals, and those in the 30-49 age group were more likely to express mental health concerns during the pandemic.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a different demographic pattern (specifying sex, race, and a 30-49 age group) versus the preprint's broader 'middle-aged and older adults' claim, so the demographic claim was revised and narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The COVID-19 pandemic has noticeable effects on mental health concerns on Twitter in the UK, which varied among demographic and geographic groups.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The COVID-19 pandemic has a significant impact on mental health concerns on Twitter in the US.
alignments.rationale: The overall conclusion that the pandemic impacted Twitter mental health concerns is preserved, but the publication focuses on the US rather than the UK and reports different subgroup patterns, so the claim is revised.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Topic modeling revealed that 'stay-at-home,' 'death poll,' and 'politics and policy' were the most popular topics in COVID-19 mental health tweets.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes topic-modeling results that were not present in the preprint, so this is an added claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Twitter users from the east and west coast had more mental health concerns during the COVID-19 pandemic.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports specific US coastal geographic differences that were not in the preprint (which focused on UK geography), so this is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model achieved an accuracy of 0.97 on 264 internal testing mammograms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model achieved 0.97 accuracy and 1.00 area under the receiver operator characteristic curve on 264 internal testing mammograms.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the reported 0.97 accuracy and adds a perfect AUC (1.00), which clarifies model performance and amplifies the apparent diagnostic performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieved an accuracy of 0.82 on 70 external testing mammograms.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the stated external-test accuracy on 70 mammograms; that preprint result appears to be omitted in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study confirmed the feasibility of using a DL model for fat-infiltrated LN classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study confirmed the feasibility of using a DL model for fat-infiltrated LN classification.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same feasibility conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model provides a practical tool to identify fatty LNs on mammograms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model provides a practical tool to identify fatty LNs on mammograms.
alignments.rationale: Both versions make the identical claim about the model being a practical identification tool for fatty LNs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our study is the first to classify fatty LNs using an automated DL approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our study is the first to classify fatty LNs using an automated DL approach.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the novelty claim that this is the first automated DL classification of fatty LNs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified key alterations in facial expression that reliably signal changes in mood state across all individuals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified key alterations in facial expression that reliably signal changes in mood state across all individuals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication statement is virtually identical in content and certainty to the preprint claim, so there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our DISC-based classifiers deliver substantially better predictions compared with commercial deep learning solutions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our DISC-based classifiers deliver substantially better predictions compared with commercial deep learning solutions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about comparative performance is repeated verbatim in the publication with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DISC-based facial analysis can be used to reliably identify an individual’s emotion. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate that DISC-based facial analysis can be used to reliably identify an individual's emotion. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion (adding the phrase 'We demonstrate' does not change meaning or certainty).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our DISC-based classifiers are inherently free of racial or gender bias. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DISC-based classifiers are inherently free of racial or gender bias. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The bias-related conclusion is maintained verbatim in the publication with no apparent qualification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We developed a highly sensitive, noninvasive, noncontact emotional state assessment tool. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not repeat this positive contribution claim and instead adds a limitation about sample size and participant awareness, so the original contribution claim is omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our sample size was limited, and participants were aware their faces were recorded on video. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This limitation appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DL models predicted CMS with high classification performance in multiple comparative analyses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DL models predicted CMS with high classification performance in multiple comparative analyses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim without modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cases with WSIs classified as imCMS1 had a significantly higher likelihood of achieving pCR (OR=2.69, 95%CI 1.01-7.17, p=0.048). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cases with WSIs classified as imCMS1 had a significantly higher likelihood of achieving pCR (OR = 2.69, 95% CI 1.01–7.17, p = 0.048). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical result and interpretation are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: imCMS4 was associated with lack of pCR (OR=0.25, 95%CI 0.07-0.88, p=0.031). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: imCMS4 was associated with lack of pCR (OR = 0.25, 95% CI 0.07–0.88, p = 0.031). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The association, effect size, and significance are preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: imCMS classification of pre-treatment biopsies is a fast and inexpensive solution to identify patient groups that could benefit from neoadjuvant LCRT. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: imCMS classification of pre-treatment biopsies is a fast and inexpensive solution to identify patient groups that could benefit from neoadjuvant LCRT. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The translational conclusion is carried forward identically into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The significant associations between imCMS1/imCMS4 with pCR suggest the existence of predictive morphological features that could enhance standard pathological assessment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The significant associations between imCMS1/imCMS4 with pCR suggest the existence of predictive morphological features that could enhance standard pathological assessment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This interpretive conclusion appears unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The customized sequential deep learning model predicts the risk of childhood obesity using only routinely collected EHR data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model can predict the risk of obesity for young children at multiple time points using only routinely collected electronic health record data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert prediction using only routinely collected EHR data; the publication frames it as ability across multiple time points but retains the same core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieved an area under the curve (AUROC) of above 0.8, with most cases around 0.9, for predicting obesity within the next 3 years for children aged 2 to 7. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed predictive model achieved an AUROC above 0.8 in all cases for predicting obesity within the next 3 years for children aged 2-7 years. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes that AUROC was >0.8 in all cases, while the preprint noted most were ~0.9; the core performance claim is preserved but the publication presents a clearer uniform statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The validation results demonstrate the robustness of the model across various subgroups and settings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model's validation results demonstrate its robustness and that its top predictors align with important risk factors of obesity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the claim of robustness in validation; it additionally couples this to predictor interpretation but does not contradict the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The most influential predictors identified by the model align with important risk factors of obesity. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model's validation results demonstrate its robustness and that its top predictors align with important risk factors of obesity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication combines predictor alignment with validation and presents the point at high certainty; the content is retained and presented with stronger framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can serve as an objective screening tool to inform prevention efforts in primary care settings. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model can serve as an objective screening tool to provide clinicians with insights into a patient's risk for developing obesity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The practical clinical application is preserved, with slightly different wording but equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sex was associated with differences in right ventricle volumes and ejection fraction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sex was associated with differences in right ventricle volumes and ejection fraction after adjusting for various factors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies that the sex differences persist after adjustment, which refines but does not change the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Six genome-wide significant loci revealed heterogeneity of allelic effects between women and men. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Six genome-wide significant loci revealed heterogeneity of allelic effects between women and men, indicating significant sex-by-genotype interaction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds explicit interpretation (sex-by-genotype interaction) but the underlying finding of heterogeneous allelic effects is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A locus for right ventricle ejection fraction in BMPR1A and a locus for right ventricle end-systolic volume near DMRT2 are present in women only. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A variant located in the promoter of BMPR1A was significantly associated with cardiac index in female patients with pulmonary arterial hypertension. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a BMPR1A promoter variant association but in a different phenotype and cohort (female PAH patients) and omits the DMRT2 locus, indicating the original broad female-only loci claim was narrowed and specified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Variation at the BMPR1A locus likely alters transcriptional regulation in right ventricle tissue. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not assert the proposed regulatory mechanism for BMPR1A variation; that mechanistic claim from the preprint is not present in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BMPR1A has emerged as a biologically plausible candidate gene for female-specific genetic determination of right ventricle function. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BMPR1A has emerged as a biologically plausible candidate gene for female-specific genetic determination of right ventricle function. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the candidate-gene interpretation but presents it with somewhat reduced certainty, so the content is the same but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best generated deep transfer learning model, named HC, achieved an AUC of 0.909 when applied to the CT dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best generated deep transfer learning model, named HC, achieved an AUC of 0.909 when applied to the CT dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is repeated verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ConvNeXt yielded a higher marginal AUC of 0.933 compared to 0.93 for HX when considering the X-ray dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ConvNeXt yielded a higher marginal AUC of 0.933 compared to 0.93 for HX when considering the X-ray dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the comparative AUCs identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The presented work is the first to expand the model space and identify a better performing model among 10,000 constructed deep transfer learning models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The presented work is the first to expand the model space and identify a better performing model among 10,000 constructed deep transfer learning models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The novelty claim is preserved unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The early and highly accurate prediction of COVID-19 based on medical images can speed up the diagnostic process and mitigate disease spread. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The early and highly accurate prediction of COVID-19 based on medical images can speed up the diagnostic process and mitigate disease spread. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The translational implication is reiterated without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predictions relying on longitudinal data were more accurate than those relying solely on admission data.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or restate a comparison between longitudinal vs admission-only predictions; this specific result is absent in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Complete case analyses from 19 predictors showed good discrimination (AUC > 0.77) to predict death between 12 and 24 hours onwards.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Complete-case analyses from 19 predictors showed good discrimination (AUC > 0.77) to predict death between 12 and 24 hours onward.
alignments.rationale: Text and statistics are effectively identical between preprint and publication, indicating the result was retained without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multiple imputation allowed to include 70 predictors and keep 95% of patients, with similar performances to complete case analyses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multiple imputation allowed us to include 70 predictors and keep 95% of patients, with similar performances.
alignments.rationale: The content is the same but the publication expresses the finding with higher certainty; the substantive claim about multiple imputation and similar performance remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This proof of concept supports that automated analysis of electronic health records can be of great interest throughout patients’ stays.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This proof-of-concept study supports that automated analysis of electronic health records can be of great interest throughout patients’ stays as a surveillance tool.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same conclusion but adds the specific framing of the application as a "surveillance tool," which clarifies intended use.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reduced CMPAase and increased AREase activities are significantly associated with acute ischemic stroke (AIS) and modified Rankin score (mRS) and NIHSS scores.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reduced CMPAase and increased AREase activities are significantly associated with acute ischemic stroke (AIS) and modified Rankin score (mRS) and NIHSS scores.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this association with the same wording and level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best predictor of AIS/disabilities was a decrease in the z-unit-based composite zCMPAase-zAREase score.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best predictor of AIS and disabilities was a decrease in the z-unit-based composite zCMPAase-zAREase score.
alignments.rationale: The predictor claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Serum high density lipoprotein cholesterol (HDL) was significantly correlated with CMPAase, but not AREase, activity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Serum high density lipoprotein cholesterol (HDLc) was significantly correlated with CMPAase activity, but not AREase activity.
alignments.rationale: The correlation claim is the same, but the publication reports it with higher certainty (medium → high), indicating amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Regression analysis showed that 34.7% of the variance in baseline NIHSS was explained by zCMPAase-zAREase and zCMPAase+zHDL composites, HDL, and hypertension.
alignments.rationale: The specific regression variance decomposition (34.7%) and predictors listed do not appear among the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The PON1 Q192R genotype has many significant direct and mediated effects on AIS/disabilities, however, its overall effect was not significant.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PON1 Q192R genotype has many significant direct and mediated effects on AIS and disabilities, however, its overall effect was not significant.
alignments.rationale: The statement about the genotype's multiple effects but nonsignificant overall impact is preserved between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individuals with ASD showed a significant increase in theta band power during social interaction compared to resting state, unlike individuals with TD.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individuals with ASD showed a significant increase in theta band power over the cerebellum and the frontal and temporal cortices during social interaction compared to resting state.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the ASD increase during social interaction vs rest but drops the explicit contrast with TD (and adds regional specificity), so the original comparative claim was revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ASD group exhibited higher theta power over the cerebellum and the frontal and temporal cortices compared to the TD group.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states it did not find significant differences between ASD and TD, so this preprint claim of group differences is not supported and is removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There were bilateral connectivity alterations between the cerebellum and the sensorimotor and parietal cortices in individuals with ASD.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There were significant coherence increases between the cerebellum and the sensorimotor, frontal and parietal cortices in individuals with ASD during social interaction.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that connectivity changes were significant coherence increases during social interaction and additionally mentions frontal cortex involvement, broadening and clarifying the original connectivity claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study uncovered ASD-specific alterations in the theta dynamics, especially in a network between the cerebellum and social-associated cortical networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The exploratory study emphasizes the need for complementary functional connectivity measures to capture network-level alterations.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's stronger claim of having uncovered ASD-specific theta network alterations is softened in the publication, which frames the work as exploratory and calls for complementary measures, reducing confidence and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Logistic regression for mortality prediction results in the highest area under the curve (AUC) of 0.795.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Logistic regression for mortality prediction results in the highest area under the curve (AUC) of 0.795.
alignments.rationale: The result is reproduced identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Logistic regression for LMClass prediction achieves the highest accuracy of 0.630.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Logistic regression for LMClass prediction achieves the highest accuracy of 0.630.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this performance metric without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Random forests (RF) outperforms other methods in length-of-stay (LOS) prediction with the highest AUC of 0.689.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Random forests (RF) outperforms other methods in length-of-stay (LOS) prediction with the highest AUC of 0.689.
alignments.rationale: The claim and reported metric are unchanged in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MODS and NEMS, as well as their components measured upon patient arrival, significantly contribute to health outcome prediction in ICUs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MODS and NEMS, as well as their components measured upon patient arrival, significantly contribute to health outcome prediction in ICUs.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about MODS and NEMS contributions is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Rapid Nanopore sequencing can provide a sparse methylation profile during surgery for intraoperative diagnostics.
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not explicitly mention providing a sparse methylation profile during surgery; that specific technical framing is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Sturgeon neural-network approach delivers accurate and fast diagnoses with as little as 20 to 40 minutes of sequencing data in 45 out of 49 pediatric samples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sturgeon delivered an accurate diagnosis within 40 minutes after starting sequencing in 45 out of 50 retrospectively sequenced samples.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core performance claim but clarifies timing as within 40 minutes and slightly updates the sample count; the main diagnostic-speed claim remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In four intraoperative cases, a turnaround time of 60-90 minutes from sample biopsy to result was achieved, impacting surgical decision making.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sturgeon achieved a diagnostic turnaround time of less than 90 minutes during 25 surgeries.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a larger number of surgeries with sub-90-minute turnaround (25 vs 4), broadening and strengthening the intraoperative turnaround claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine-learned diagnosis based on intraoperative sequencing can assist neurosurgical decision making, potentially avoiding neurological comorbidity or preventing additional surgeries.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine-learned diagnosis based on low-cost intraoperative sequencing can assist neurosurgical decision-making.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the assistance claim but removes the specific downstream clinical impact phrasing (avoiding comorbidity/preventing additional surgeries), narrowing and slightly clarifying the conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ConvNeXt Tiny model achieved an accuracy of 95% in identifying malaria parasites in blood smear images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ConvNeXt Tiny model, version V1 Tiny, achieved an accuracy of 95.9%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the reported accuracy and specifies the version (95% -> 95.9%), preserving the same result claim but with a clearer value and version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The re-modified version of the ConvNeXt V2 Tiny model reached an accuracy of 98% in malaria detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The upgraded V2 Tiny Remod version exceeded this benchmark, reaching 98.1% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives a slightly more precise accuracy (98% -> 98.1%) while keeping the same claim about the upgraded V2 Tiny model.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates the potential for implementing ConvNeXt-based systems in regions with limited healthcare resources for malaria diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings highlight the potential for real-time diagnostic applications in remote healthcare facilities. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the implementation potential specifically as real-time applications in remote facilities (more specific) and uses slightly lower certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of label smoothing with the AdamW optimiser produced a model which exhibited strong robustness as well as generalisability. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This methodological/robustness result appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The enhanced ConvNeXt V2 Tiny model combined with data augmentation, transfer learning techniques and explainability frameworks demonstrates a practical solution for malaria diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a claim about combining techniques to form a practical solution, which does not have an explicit counterpart in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The selective ensemble methods improved segmentation performance with DSCs of up to 93.11%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The selective ensemble methods improved the segmentation performance with DSCs up to 93.07%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a nearly identical DSC value (minor numeric adjustment) and the same improvement claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods provided a better delineation of lumen boundaries near the coronary lesion with local DSCs of up to 94.04%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific local DSC claim and lumen-boundary delineation result do not appear in the publication; there is no clear matching statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The probability of mask disconnection at the most narrowed region could be minimized to less than 1%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methods minimized the chances of mask disconnection in the most narrowed regions to 2.10%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a different numeric outcome (worse than <1%), representing a substantive revision of the reported failure rate while keeping the same focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inference time for major vessel segmentation was approximately one-third, indicating potential for real-time application in clinical settings. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results suggest better applicability of real-time QCA-based diagnostic methods in routine clinical settings. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the real-time applicability claim (removing the detailed 'one-third' timing) and expresses it with lower certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The robustness of the proposed methods was evident in the external validation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit external validation/robustness claim that was not stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Peripheral blood cells are more deformable in patients with depressive disorders compared to healthy controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Peripheral blood cells are more deformable in patients with depressive disorders compared to healthy controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same overall result claim with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lifetime persistent depressive disorder is associated with increased cell deformability in monocytes and neutrophils. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lifetime persistent depressive disorder is associated with increased cell deformability in monocytes and neutrophils. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific association is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current persistent depressive disorder is associated with increased deformability of erythrocytes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim corresponds to increased erythrocyte deformability for current persistent depressive disorder; that specific result is not reported.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lymphocytes are more deformable in current major depressive disorder, while no differences are identified for lifetime major depressive disorder. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lymphocytes, monocytes, and neutrophils are the major blood cells affected by increased deformability in depressive disorders. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the finding to multiple immune cell types and does not preserve the preprint's current-vs-lifetime distinction, broadening and revising the original nuance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study highlights that depressive disorders, particularly persistent depressive disorders, are associated with increased blood cell deformability. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Peripheral blood cells are more deformable in patients with depressive disorders compared to healthy controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the main conclusion that depressive disorders associate with increased blood cell deformability, matching the preprint's conclusion.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This study is the first to analyze morpho-rheological properties of entire blood cells in relation to depressive disorders. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a novelty/contribution statement that was not present as a distinct claim in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Immune cell mechanical changes in depressive disorders might be predictive of persistent immune response. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a tentative predictive/interpretative conclusion about immune response that does not appear in the preprint claims list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieves 98.49% accuracy in classifying COVID-19 from CT scan images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system achieved 98.49% accuracy in single image classification on more than 7996 test images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the exact accuracy figure and adds dataset context (test image count), clarifying the reported result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system correctly identified almost 234 of 245 patients with high speed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system correctly identified 234 out of 245 patients during the patient condition identification phase. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this patient-level identification result verbatim with equal certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel method for increasing the classification accuracy of convolutional networks is introduced. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel architecture applied to the ResNet50V2 model significantly improves classification accuracy for images containing small important objects. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the architecture (ResNet50V2) and the context (small important objects) and reports the contribution with higher certainty, amplifying the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system reduces processing time and false detections by discarding CT images where the lung is not properly visible. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates high-speed and accurate detection of COVID-19 from chest CT scan images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes overall high-speed and accuracy, which aligns with but generalizes the preprint's specific mechanism for reducing processing time and false detections.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset introduced in this study is accessible for further research. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a data-access/contribution statement that was not in the preprint claims list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Smartphone-based ambulatory severity outcomes could accurately estimate MS level of disability, as measured by the EDSS score. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Smartphone-based ambulatory severity outcomes could accurately estimate MS level of disability, as measured by the EDSS score. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result claim with identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Longitudinal severity outcomes were shown to accurately reflect individual participants’ level of disability over the study duration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Longitudinal severity outcomes were shown to accurately reflect individual participants' level of disability over the study duration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this longitudinal result identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Smartphone-based assessments could greatly augment standard in-clinic outcomes for neurodegenerative diseases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Smartphone-based assessments could greatly augment standard in-clinic outcomes for neurodegenerative diseases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this conclusion with the same wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ability to understand the impact of disease on daily life between clinical visits through objective digital outcomes paves the way forward to better measure and identify signs of disease progression. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ability to understand the impact of disease on daily life between clinical visits paves the way forward to better measure and identify signs of disease progression. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion verbatim with unchanged certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model based on T1-CE alone achieved the best segmentation performance for brain metastasis segmentation with a median Dice similarity coefficient of 0.96.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model based on T1-CE alone achieved the best segmentation performance for brain metastasis segmentation with a median Dice similarity coefficient of 0.96.
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication texts are identical in content and metrics, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models trained without T1-CE performed worse, with T1-only achieving a Dice similarity coefficient of 0.70 and T2-FLAIR-only achieving 0.73.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models trained without T1-CE performed worse, with T1-only achieving a Dice similarity coefficient of 0.70 and T2-FLAIR-only achieving 0.73.
alignments.rationale: The performance numbers and comparative claim are the same in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of T1-CE and T2-FLAIR is important for edema segmentation, achieving a Dice similarity coefficient of 0.93.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of T1-CE and T2-FLAIR is important for edema segmentation, achieving a Dice similarity coefficient of 0.93.
alignments.rationale: Claim and reported metric are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Missing either T1-CE or T2-FLAIR decreases performance in segmentation tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Missing either T1-CE or T2-FLAIR decreases performance in segmentation tasks.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about decreased performance when sequences are missing is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings may improve imaging routines by omitting unnecessary sequences, allowing for faster procedures in daily clinical practice.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings may improve imaging routines by omitting unnecessary sequences, allowing for faster procedures in daily clinical practice.
alignments.rationale: The practical implication statement appears verbatim and unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sCCfC model achieves an F1 score and AUROC of 0.82 and 0.91 in cardiac abnormalities detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The sCCfC model achieves an F1 score and AUROC of 0.82 and 0.91 in cardiac abnormalities detection.
alignments.rationale: Identical performance metrics and claim in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sCCfC model demonstrates a significantly higher energy efficiency with an estimated power consumption of 4.68 µ J/Inf on an emulated Loihi’s neuromorphic chip architecture.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The sCCfC model demonstrates a significantly higher energy efficiency with an estimated power consumption of 4.68 μJ/Inf on an emulated Loihi’s neuromorphic chip architecture.
alignments.rationale: The claim is the same though micro-unit notation differs; meaning and value are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: After initial training of 2 epochs on a conventional GPU, the F1 score and AUROC improved from 0.46 and 0.65 to 0.56 and 0.73 respectively with 5 additional epochs of on-device training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: After initial training of two epochs on a conventional Graphics Processing Unit, the F1 score and AUROC improved from 0.46 and 0.65 to 0.56 and 0.73, respectively, with five additional epochs of on-device training.
alignments.rationale: Same numerical improvements and training procedure described, only minor wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sCCfC model showcases strong out-of-sample generalization capabilities, achieving an F1 score and AUROC of 0.71 and 0.86 when presented with a new dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The sCCfC model showcases strong out-of-sample generalization capabilities, achieving an F1 score and AUROC of 0.71 and 0.86, respectively, when presented with a new dataset.
alignments.rationale: The reported generalization metrics and claim are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The spiking sCCfC outperforms the non-spiking ConvCfC model in robustness regarding effectively handling missing ECG channels during inference.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The spiking sCCfC outperforms the non-spiking ConvCfC model in robustness regarding effectively handling missing electrocardiogram (ECG) channels during inference.
alignments.rationale: Content is the same; publication expands acronym (ECG) but meaning is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individuals with Body Integrity Dysphoria (BID) reported a higher sense of ownership and control over the virtual body.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individuals with Body Integrity Dysphoria (BID) reported a higher sense of ownership and control over the virtual body.
alignments.rationale: The experiential report is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Task-based fMRI showed increased pre-VR activity in the right superior parietal lobule, right angular gyrus, and right supplementary motor area in the BID group, normalizing after VR exposure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Task-based fMRI showed increased pre-VR activity in the right superior parietal lobule, right angular gyrus, and right supplementary motor area in the BID group, normalizing after VR exposure.
alignments.rationale: Neuroimaging findings and their normalization after VR are described the same way.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Resting-state fMRI showed reduced connectivity in the right superior parietal lobule and other networks, normalizing post-VR.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Resting-state fMRI showed reduced connectivity in the right superior parietal lobule and other networks, normalizing post-VR.
alignments.rationale: Resting-state connectivity findings are unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Virtual embodiment effectively modulates BID-related neural networks, offering a safe, cost-effective intervention for BID.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Virtual embodiment effectively modulates BID-related neural networks, offering a safe, cost-effective intervention for BID.
alignments.rationale: The interpretation about intervention potential is identical in both texts.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings highlight VR’s potential in exploring the complex interaction between body and self, with implications for similar psychiatric conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings highlight VR's potential in exploring the complex interaction between body and self, with implications for similar psychiatric conditions.
