alignments.rationale: Broad implications for VR are stated the same, only minor punctuation differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our proposed network exhibits robust performance across different datasets through rigorous training and validation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our proposed network exhibits robust performance across different datasets through rigorous training and validation.
alignments.rationale: High-level claim about robustness is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We achieved AUROC scores of 81.0% and 91.0% in two datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We achieved AUROC scores of 81.0% and 91.0% in two datasets.
alignments.rationale: The numeric AUROC results are identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We obtained AUPRC and F1 Score metrics of 91.9% and 88.9% for one dataset, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We obtained area under the precision-recall curve and F1 score metrics of 91.9% and 88.9% for one dataset, respectively.
alignments.rationale: Metrics and values match exactly; publication expands acronyms but retains same meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model highlights its effectiveness across continental datasets with diverse brain modalities, regardless of montage or age specificity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model highlights its effectiveness across continental datasets with diverse brain modalities, regardless of montage or age specificity.
alignments.rationale: Broad generalizability claim is consistent between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach eliminates the need for computationally expensive feature engineering techniques like Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) and STFT.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach eliminates the need for computationally expensive feature engineering techniques like fast Fourier transform and STFT.
alignments.rationale: Claim about avoiding specific feature-engineering techniques is unchanged; only capitalization differs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: U-Sleep performance is statistically equivalent to trained human scorers in pediatric sleep staging.
alignments.pub_claim_text: U-Sleep demonstrated statistically equivalent performance to trained human scorers in pediatric sleep staging.
alignments.rationale: The equivalence claim is present in both versions with only minor wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The median kappa agreement between U-Sleep and clinical sleep-staging is 0.69.
alignments.rationale: The specific median kappa value reported in the preprint does not appear among the publication claims provided; therefore the numeric claim is treated as removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: U-Sleep accuracy is lower for children under 2 years old and those with sleep-disordered breathing or comorbidities affecting electroencephalography.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of U-Sleep was lower in children under 2 years of age and in those with sleep-disordered breathing or comorbidities affecting electroencephalography.
alignments.rationale: The subgroup accuracy limitation is stated the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: U-Sleep is suitable for pediatric clinical utilization if automated staging is followed by expert clinician review.
alignments.pub_claim_text: U-Sleep is suitable for pediatric clinical utilization if automated staging is followed by expert clinician review.
alignments.rationale: The recommended clinical application and caveat are identical in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The added path length, false negative path length, and surface Dice similarity coefficient correlated better with correction time than traditional metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The added path length, false negative path length, and surface Dice similarity coefficient correlated better with correction time than traditional metrics, including the volumetric Dice similarity coefficient. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result but explicitly names the volumetric Dice as an example of 'traditional metrics', which clarifies rather than changes the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clinical variables poorly represented in the autosegmentation tool's training data were often associated with decreased accuracy but not necessarily with prolonged correction time. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clinical variables poorly represented in the autosegmentation tool’s training data were often associated with decreased accuracy but not necessarily with prolonged correction time. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same observation with essentially identical wording and implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Metrics used to develop and evaluate autosegmentation tools should correlate with clinical time saved. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Metrics used to develop and evaluate autosegmentation tools should correlate with clinical time saved. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The published claim is the same recommendation but expressed with higher certainty, so it clarifies and amplifies the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Novel spatial similarity metrics may be preferable to traditional metrics for developing and evaluating autosegmentation tools that are intended to save clinicians time. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Novel spatial similarity metrics may be preferable to traditional metrics for developing and evaluating autosegmentation tools that are intended to save clinicians time. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Wording is the same but the publication expresses the claim with lower certainty, so the semantic content is unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Dynamic Deformable Attention Network (DDANet) improves the performance on semantic segmentation tasks compared to criss-cross attention within a U-Net on a challenging COVID-19 lesion segmentation task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Dynamic Deformable Attention Network (DDANet) improves the performance on semantic segmentation tasks compared to criss-cross attention within a U-Net on a challenging COVID-19 lesion segmentation task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DDANet achieves Dice scores of 73.4% and 61.3% for Ground-Glass-Opacity and Consolidation lesions for COVID-19 segmentation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DDANet achieves Dice scores of 73.4% and 61.3% for Ground-glass opacity and consolidation lesions for COVID-19 segmentation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical numeric results and lesion labels; only minor capitalization differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance gain of the recursively applied dynamic deformable attention blocks comes from their ability to capture dynamic and precise (wider) attention context. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance gain of the recursively applied dynamic deformable attention blocks comes from their ability to capture dynamic and precise attention context. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same explanatory conclusion about the attention blocks' mechanism; wording is effectively unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DDANet improves the accuracy by 4.9% points compared to a baseline U-Net. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DDANet improves the accuracy by 4.9% points compared to a baseline U-Net and 24.4% points compared to current state of art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the original baseline comparison and adds an extra comparison to state-of-the-art methods, broadening the scope of the performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The non-contact system developed can continuously determine an individual’s position in bed with 98.1% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The non-contact system developed can determine an individual's position in bed with 94.2% accuracy in a lab environment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published result reports a lower accuracy and adds the constraint of a lab environment, revising and narrowing the original performance claim and tempering its effectiveness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system demonstrated an F1 score of 0.982 using the XGBoost classifier. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system was able to detect the position of nine participants in the home environment with an F1 score of 0.982. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The F1 score is the same but the publication specifies it pertains to nine participants in the home environment, adding context and narrowing generality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adherence to repositioning schedules for pressure injury prevention remains poor in clinical and home environments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This conclusion about adherence is not reflected in the published claims; it appears omitted from the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future work will include using this system to evaluate interventions aimed at improving adherence to 2-hour repositioning schedules. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The specific future-work item about evaluating interventions for 2-hour repositioning is not present in the publication, which instead lists different future directions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A total of 695 and 392 significant microbial associations at different taxonomic levels were found with incident asthma and COPD, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A total of 695 and 392 statistically significant associations were found between baseline taxonomic groups and incident asthma and COPD, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same numerical findings with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gradient boosting decision trees of baseline gut microbiome predicted incident asthma and COPD with mean area under the curves of 0.608 and 0.780, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gradient boosting decision trees of baseline gut microbiome abundance predicted incident asthma and COPD in the validation data sets with mean area under the curves of 0.608 and 0.780, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same predictive performance reported; publication adds 'validation data sets' but does not change substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The baseline gut microbiome had C-indices of 0.623 for asthma and 0.817 for COPD, which were more predictive than other conventional risk factors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The baseline gut microbiome achieved higher predictive performance than individual conventional risk factors, with C-indices of 0.623 for asthma and 0.817 for COPD. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Equivalent claim about predictive performance relative to conventional risk factors with identical metrics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The gut microbiome is a significant risk factor for incident asthma and incident COPD and is largely independent of conventional risk factors. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The gut microbiome is a significant risk factor for incident asthma and incident COPD and is largely independent of conventional risk factors. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Amyloid load is positively correlated with LF thickness at the levels of surgical decompression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Amyloid load is positively correlated with LF thickness at the levels of surgical decompression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim matches the preprint claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Amyloid load is positively correlated with lumbar LF burden across all lumbar levels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Amyloid load is positively correlated with lumbar LF burden across all lumbar levels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical result reported in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The correlation between amyloid load and LF thickness is dose-dependent. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The correlation between amyloid load and LF thickness is dose-dependent. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same conclusion presented with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further studies are needed to validate these findings and uncover the underlying pathophysiology. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Further studies are needed to validate these findings and uncover the underlying pathophysiology. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation statement is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neutrophils from people with rheumatoid arthritis (RA) exhibit a divergent metabolic pattern compared to neutrophils from healthy controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neutrophils from people with rheumatoid arthritis (RA) exhibit a divergent metabolic pattern compared to neutrophils from healthy controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is textually and conceptually identical to the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: JAK inhibitors significantly inhibit reactive oxygen species (ROS) production in neutrophils. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: JAK inhibitors significantly inhibit reactive oxygen species (ROS) production in neutrophils. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim preserved verbatim with same scope and confidence in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Baricitinib decreases NET production in neutrophils from people with RA. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Baricitinib decreases NET production in neutrophils. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication reports the same experimental result; wording is slightly broader (drops explicit RA phrase) but intent and finding remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effect of JAK inhibitors on neutrophils can inhibit joint damage in RA without impairing host defense. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effect of JAK inhibitors on neutrophils can inhibit joint damage in RA without impairing host defense. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion is carried over unchanged in wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Altered energy metabolism in RA neutrophils may explain their dysregulation in inflammatory disease. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Altered energy metabolism in RA neutrophils may explain their dysregulation in inflammatory disease. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The explanatory/interpretative claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Proteomic-based classifiers identified malignancy with an area under the receiver operating characteristic (AUROC) of 0.94. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Proteomic-based classifiers identified malignancy with an area under the receiver operating characteristic (AUROC) of 0.94. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported performance metric is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study recovered 755 unique proteins from cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) samples across 73 samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific methodological/result detail (755 unique proteins across 73 samples) is not present among the publication claims and appears to have been omitted from the published claim list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Novel biomarkers, including GAP43, TFF3, and CACNA2D2, were identified and characterized using single-cell RNA sequencing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Novel biomarkers were identified, including GAP43, TFF3, and CACNA2D2. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the biomarker identification claim (same markers listed); single-cell RNA sequencing detail is not contradicted and the core result is retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reliable classification of intra-axial malignancies using low CSF volumes is feasible, allowing for longitudinal tumor surveillance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reliable classification of intra-axial malignancies using low CSF volumes is feasible. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim is retained but the publication expresses higher certainty (medium -> high) and drops the explicit mention of longitudinal surveillance; core feasibility claim remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Upfront implantation of CSF reservoirs in brain tumor patients warrants consideration based on emerging evidence. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The recommendation regarding upfront CSF reservoir implantation is not present among publication claims and appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CDOT test demonstrated a specificity of 94% and a sensitivity of 90% for OSCC and 84.2% for OPSCC in the validation cohort. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The specificity of the CDOT test was 94%, and the sensitivity was 90% for participants with OSCC and 84.2% for participants with OPSCC. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numerical performance metrics are identical and retained in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CDOT is a non-invasive test that can be easily administered in various healthcare settings for early detection of OPSCC and OSCC. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CDOT is a non-invasive test that can be easily administered in dentist offices, primary care centres, and specialised cancer clinics for early detection of OPSCC and OSCC. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies particular settings (dentist offices, primary care, specialised clinics) rather than the broader phrase 'various healthcare settings', which clarifies and narrows the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CDOT test has received breakthrough designation by the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CDOT test has received FDA's breakthrough designation for accelerated review. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication retains the breakthrough designation claim and adds the purpose (accelerated review); this clarifies but does not change the claim's scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CDOT test will enable early diagnosis of OSCC and OPSCC, potentially saving lives and significantly reducing healthcare expenditure. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CDOT test has the potential to enable early diagnosis, saving lives and significantly reducing healthcare expenditure. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Language in the publication shifts from a definitive future assertion ('will enable') to a conditional formulation ('has the potential'), tempering the strength of the claim while retaining its scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The top-down DSM/ICD categories of mood disorders are inaccurate and hinder the remodeling of case definitions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The top-down categories of mood disorders in the DSM and ICD are inaccurate and hinder the remodeling of case definitions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conceptual critique of DSM/ICD categories is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new precision nomothetic model of mood disorders has been developed, characterized by a recurrence of illness index and a new endophenotype class called Major Dysmood Disorder. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new precision nomothetic model of mood disorders has been developed, characterized by a recurrence of illness index and a new endophenotype class called Major Dysmood Disorder. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The development and characterization of the new model are presented identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Rules to compute bottom-up RADAR scores for Major Dysmood Disorder and related factors have been provided. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Rules to compute bottom-up RADAR scores for Major Dysmood Disorder, recurrence of illness, lifetime suicidal behaviors, and the phenome of mood disorders have been provided. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands and specifies the items for which RADAR scoring rules are provided (explicitly lists additional constructs), thus clarifying and broadening the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RADAR graph allows for the visualization of a patient's characteristics as an idiomatic fingerprint, facilitating the estimation of key traits and severity of the illness. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RADAR graph allows for the visualization of a patient's characteristics as an idiomatic fingerprint, facilitating the estimation of key traits and severity of the illness. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The descriptive/result claim about the RADAR graph is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Biomarker research into mood disorders should utilize the RADAR scores to analyze pan-omics data for enhancing precision models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Biomarker research into mood disorders should utilize the RADAR scores to analyze pan-omics data and enhance precision models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The recommendation to use RADAR scores in biomarker research is preserved; wording is slightly tightened but meaning is retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining multiple types of blood tests and investigating trends in blood test results over time could be more useful for selecting patients for further cancer investigation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining multiple types of blood tests and investigating trends in blood test results over time could be more useful for selecting patients for further cancer investigation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim appears verbatim and unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Trends in blood test results are more useful than symptoms or single blood test results in selecting primary care patients for cancer investigation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Trends in blood test results are more useful than symptoms or single blood test results in selecting primary care patients for cancer investigation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The comparative claim about trends versus symptoms/single tests is maintained exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed clinical prediction models will incorporate trends in blood tests to identify the risk of cancer. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed clinical prediction models will incorporate trends in blood tests to identify the risk of cancer. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim about model design is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These models will help general practitioners find patients who need referral for further investigation of cancer. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These models will help general practitioners find patients who need a referral for further investigation of cancer. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The intended clinical utility for GPs is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implementation of these models could increase cancer yield and reduce unnecessary referrals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implementation of these models could increase cancer yield and reduce unnecessary referrals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The implementation outcome claim remains the same in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Beta-diversity significantly differs between patients with long (>6 months) vs. short (≤6 months) progression-free survival (PFS). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gut bacterial signatures can predict progression-free survival (PFS) in advanced-stage non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) patients treated with anti-PD1 immunotherapy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's specific result about beta-diversity is subsumed under the publication's broader claim that gut bacterial signatures predict PFS; the publication reframes the finding at a higher, less specific level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Short PFS is associated with increased abundance of Firmicutes and Actinobacteria phyla. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Short PFS is associated with increased abundance of Firmicutes and Actinobacteria phyla. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this taxonomic association verbatim, with no substantive change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Taxonomic profiles are superior in predicting PFS with an AUC of 0.74. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Taxonomic profiles of gut microbiome perform better in predicting PFS than other methods, achieving an AUC of 0.74. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements report the same performance (AUC 0.74) and conclusion about taxonomic profiles predicting PFS.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Metabolic pathways, including Amino Acid Synthesis and Fermentation, are better predictors of PD-L1 expression with an AUC of 0.87. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Metabolic pathways related to Amino Acid Synthesis and Fermentation are better predictors of PD-L1 expression, with an AUC of 0.87. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same pathway-level predictive result with matching AUC and specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The specific metagenomic features of the gut microbiome are suggestive of immune checkpoint inhibitor efficacy and PD-L1 expression in NSCLC patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gut bacterial signatures can predict progression-free survival (PFS) in advanced-stage non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) patients treated with anti-PD1 immunotherapy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's broad interpretive conclusion about metagenomic features and ICI efficacy/PD-L1 is rephrased in the publication as specific predictive results (PFS prediction), narrowing the general conclusion to concrete predictive findings.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A stronger medical provider reduces the duration of hospital stay. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Medical providers with higher levels of cooperation reduce the duration of hospital stay. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding about provider cooperation reducing length of stay with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The overall contribution of medical cooperation to the duration of hospital stay extracted from the medical provider network using node2vec is approximately 20%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The overall contribution of medical cooperation to the duration of hospital stay extracted from the medical provider network using node2vec is approximately 20%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical quantitative contribution is reported in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The contribution of medical cooperation to the duration of hospital stay using node2vec is approximately 20 times higher than the model using strength. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The contribution of medical cooperation to the duration of hospital stay using node2vec is approximately 20 times higher than the model using strength. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The comparative magnitude between models is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Yemen’s population rose from about 26.3M to 31.1M during the seven-year analysis period. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Yemen's population rose from about 26.3 million to 31.1 million during the seven-year analysis period. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The population increase is reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Approximately 10 to 14M Yemenis may have been internally displaced during 2015-2016, which is about five times the United Nations estimates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An estimated 10 to 14 million Yemenis may have been internally displaced during 2015-2016, which is about five times the United Nations estimates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The displacement estimate and its comparison to UN estimates are consistent between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The internally displaced population had declined to 1-2M by September 2021. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The internally displaced population in Yemen declined to 1-2 million by September 2021. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported decline in displaced population is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings are subject to limitations related to data quality, model inaccuracy, and omission of migration outside Yemen. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings are subject to limitations related to data quality, model inaccuracy, and omission of migration outside Yemen. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitations are stated the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis illustrates approaches to analyzing the dynamics of displacement and the application of different models and data streams. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This methodological/concluding statement present in the preprint is not reflected in the set of publication claims, indicating it was omitted or de-emphasized.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-layer perceptron model achieved a mean AUC of 0.967 ± 0.019 in identifying delirium. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The logistic regression model achieved a mean AUC of 0.874 ± 0.033 in identifying delirium. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a different model with substantially lower AUC, indicating a revised performance claim and a tempered overall result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification model can identify delirium cases that are otherwise undocumented during routine practice. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model promises to identify delirium cases with EHR data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same conceptual conclusion that EHR-based models can identify delirium cases, but expresses it more generally as a promise rather than emphasizing undocumented cases specifically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model's mean positive predictive value (PPV) was 0.9, and the mean negative predicted value (NPV) was 0.88. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean positive predictive value of the logistic regression model was 0.80. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Predictive value metrics differ between the preprint (higher PPV and reported NPV) and the publication (lower PPV for a different model), so the claim was revised and confidence tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cohort built from this model would be useful for evaluating long-term sequelae of delirium in the ICU. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classification model enables a sustainable infrastructure to build a retrospective cohort of delirium. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the model's role in enabling infrastructure to build retrospective cohorts rather than explicitly stating the cohort's use for long-term sequelae evaluation, narrowing the original claimed utility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of transfer learning for non-image data in clinical literature has grown rapidly within the last few years. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of transfer learning for non-image data in clinical literature has grown rapidly within the last few years. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same observation about growth in transfer learning usage.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Transfer learning was most often applied to time series data (61%) in the reviewed studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Transfer learning was most often applied to time series data (61%) in the reviewed studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The quantitative breakdown and domain focus are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many studies (66%) used publicly available datasets, but fewer shared their code (27%). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many studies (66%) used publicly available datasets, but fewer shared their code (27%). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Data sharing and code-sharing statistics are reported identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The review identified studies that demonstrate the potential of transfer learning in clinical research across a wide range of medical specialties. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The review identified studies that demonstrate the potential of transfer learning in clinical research across a wide range of medical specialties. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The review's overall conclusion about potential across specialties is preserved exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: More interdisciplinary collaborations and the wider adoption of reproducible research principles are needed to increase the impact of transfer learning in clinical research. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: More interdisciplinary collaborations and the wider adoption of reproducible research principles are needed to increase the impact of transfer learning in clinical research. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The recommendation for collaborations and reproducibility is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We found six metabolites that were significantly altered in NAFLD among 79 detected metabolites. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Six metabolites were significantly altered in nonalcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) among 79 detected metabolites. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q0 repeats P0 nearly verbatim with the same scope and certainty, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Eight metabolites could distinguish the three groups of healthy controls, NAFL, and NASH. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Eight metabolites could distinguish the three groups of healthy controls, NAFL, and NASH. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q1 is effectively identical to P1 in content and strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MetaNASH score, formulated using three selected metabolites, distinguished NASH with excellent performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MetaNASH score, formulated using three selected metabolites, distinguished NASH with excellent performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q2 matches P2 verbatim, with the same performance claim and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We suggested the pathophysiologic implications of metabolite profiles in relation to NAFLD progression. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are correlations between the concentration of plasma metabolites and the expression of enzymes governing metabolism, with specific alterations in NASH. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q3 provides more specific mechanistic correlations (metabolite concentrations vs enzyme expression) than the broader pathophysiologic suggestion in P3, narrowing and clarifying the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Remote photoplethysmography (rPPG) signals are capable of dealing with motion artifacts better than contact PPG sensors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Remote photoplethysmography (rPPG) signals are capable of dealing with motion artifacts better than contact PPG sensors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q0 repeats P0 exactly on motion artifact handling and retains the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: rPPG signals have better quality compared to the signals from contact sensors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim corresponds to the broad assertion of better overall signal quality; the publication focuses on motion artifacts rather than an explicit overall quality superiority.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: At least two heart rate variability (HRV) features, NNi 20 and SAMPEN, can differentiate between stress and non-stress states. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Statistical analysis has shown that at least two heart-rate variability (HRV) features, NNi 20 and SAMPEN, can differentiate between stress and non-stress states. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q1 states the same features distinguish stress vs non-stress but frames it as the result of statistical analysis and with higher expressed certainty, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Three features, IBI, NNi 20, and SAMPEN, can differentiate between tasks relating to different levels of difficulty. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Three features, inter-beat interval (IBI), NNi 20, and SAMPEN, can differentiate between tasks relating to different levels of difficulty. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q2 repeats P3's feature list and task-difficulty discrimination but reports higher certainty (statistical backing), so the claim is unchanged in content but amplified in confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models achieved an accuracy score of 83.11% in classifying a 'stressed' or 'unstressed' state. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models achieved an accuracy score of 83.11% in classifying a 'stressed' or 'unstressed' state. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q3 matches P4 verbatim, including the numeric accuracy and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified proteomic and clinical biomarkers for AMS diagnosis, prognosis, protection, and pathogenesis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study quantified 1069 proteins and validated 102 proteins related to AMS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q2 provides concrete proteomic quantification and validation that substantiate the broader preprint claim of identified proteomic biomarkers, thereby clarifying and grounding the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RET and ADAM15 could be promising therapeutic targets for AMS. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RET played an important role in the pathogenesis of acute mountain sickness (AMS). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PHGDH and TRAF2 could be candidate predictive and diagnostic biomarkers for AMS, respectively. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ADAM15, PHGDH, and TRAF2 were prioritized as protective, predictive, and diagnostic biomarkers for AMS, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q1 explicitly prioritizes these proteins with assigned roles and higher certainty, thus clarifying and strengthening P2's candidate-biomarker claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: C-peptide was found to be actively involved in the pathogenesis and could aid the assistant diagnosis of AMS. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim mentions C-peptide's role or diagnostic utility, so this specific claim appears omitted in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individuals with AMS showed higher gluconeogenesis activity at the plain than those without AMS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state this specific metabolic finding explicitly, so the claim appears to have been removed or not reported in the final publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Prolonged QRS duration is identified as a predictor of ventricular tachycardia/ventricular fibrillation (VT/VF) in ARVC/D patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Prolonged QRS duration is identified as a predictor of ventricular tachycardia/ventricular fibrillation (VT/VF) in ARVC/D patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q0 and P0 are essentially identical in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Female gender, prolonged QTc duration, the presence of epsilon waves, and T-wave inversion in any lead except aVR/V1 are predictors of new-onset heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Female gender, prolonged QTc duration, the presence of epsilon waves, and T-wave inversion in any lead except aVR/V1 are predictors of new-onset heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q1 restates P1 with no material change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The presence of epsilon waves, along with prolonged QRS duration and worsening ejection fraction, predicts all-cause mortality in ARVC/D patients. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Clinical scores developed to predict incident VT/VF, new-onset HFrEF, and all-cause mortality were significantly improved by machine learning techniques. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PRECOG can help researchers and policymakers to carry out and critically appraise causal and counterfactual prediction model studies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper describes methods and processes that will be used to develop a Reporting Guideline for Causal and Counterfactual Prediction Models (PRECOG). (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PRECOG will be useful for designing interventions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not make the explicit claim about PRECOG's usefulness for designing interventions, so this specific anticipated use appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is an anticipated further expansion of the guideline for specific areas, e.g., pharmaceutical interventions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The published version details development stages and dissemination but does not state the anticipated expansion to specific domains, so this projection was not carried into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A complete and reliable list of risk factors from routine laboratory blood tests is important for COVID-19 disease severity and mortality. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A complete and reliable list of risk factors from routine laboratory blood tests is important for COVID-19 disease severity and mortality. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method produces a more robust list of risk factors by running multiple analyses on the same set of data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method produces a more robust list of risk factors by running multiple analyses on the same set of data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Twenty out of thirty factors are deemed to be reliably associated with faster-death or faster-recovery. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 20 out of 30 factors are deemed to be reliably associated with faster-death or faster-recovery. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ten to fifteen factors seem to achieve optimal prognosis performance considering the correlation among factors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 10 to 15 factors seem to be able to achieve the optimal prognosis performance considering correlation among factors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same range and conclusion about optimal factor number and correlations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The final list of risk factors includes calcium, white blood cell and neutrophils count, urea and creatine, d-dimer, red cell distribution widths, age, ferritin, glucose, lactate dehydrogenase, lymphocyte, basophils, and anemia related factors. (certainty=high, type=result)

(certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two neuroanatomical signatures of schizophrenia were successfully replicated. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two neuroanatomical signatures of schizophrenia were replicated. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Signature 1 was significantly more common in youth with psychosis-spectrum symptoms than in typically developing youth. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Signature 1 was significantly more common in youths with psychosis spectrum symptoms than in typically developing youths. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: In both youths and adults, signature 1 was associated with worse cognitive performance than signature 2. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Adults expressing signature 1 had elevated schizophrenia polygenic risk scores compared with adults with neither signature. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified and quantified 4,480 unique proteins and 2,682 unique phosphoproteins from urinary extracellular vesicles. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Classification performance from in-lab actigraphic data reached an accuracy of 92.9±8.16% with a sensitivity of 94.9±7.4% and specificity of 92.7±13.8%. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When tested on home recordings, accuracy rose to 100% over the two-week window. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Wrist actigraphy can enable automatic diagnoses of rapid eye movement sleep behavior disorder (RBD) in home settings. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The number of relevant predictors for achieving home vs. community ambulation was reduced from 25 to 7. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The number of relevant predictors for achieving home vs. community ambulation was reduced from 25 to 7. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Models were trained and tested on 198,174 patients, of whom 8% were hospitalized or died within 30 days of testing positive. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: AUCs for the full models ranged from 0.80 (hospitalization) to 0.91 (death). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Translation of knowledge graphs into clinical practice remains a challenge. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Large-scale plasma proteomics with data-driven and literature-based protein selection modestly improves prediction of future major cardiovascular events (MACEs) beyond traditional risk factors. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hypothesis-driven protein selection also improved risk prediction beyond traditional risk factors, while a random selection of proteins did not. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the specific comparison stating hypothesis-driven selection improved while random selection did not, indicating this claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The median balanced accuracy for 39 models in the Finnish test data set was 99.9%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The median balanced accuracy for 39 models in the Finnish test data set was 99.9%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported median balanced accuracy is identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 34 out of 39 Finnish models were replicated in the Danish cohort with a median balanced accuracy of 97.1%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 34 out of 39 Finnish models were replicated in the Danish cohort with a median balanced accuracy of 97.1%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The replication result and metric are unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The median balanced accuracy for the 40 Danish models in the Danish test data set was 99.3%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The median balanced accuracy for the 40 Danish models in the Danish test data set was 99.3%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The Danish models' median balanced accuracy is reported identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RBC antigen and HPA-1 prediction models demonstrated high overall accuracies suitable for probabilistic determination of blood groups and HPA-1 at biobank-scale. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RBC antigen and HPA-1 prediction models demonstrated high overall accuracies suitable for probabilistic determination of blood groups and HPA-1 at biobank-scale. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding statement about model suitability is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Population-specific training cohorts increased the accuracies of the models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Population-specific training cohorts increased the accuracies of the models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion regarding population-specific training is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models that combined five and more features showed a macro area under the curve (AUC) of 0.85 to predict 90 day mortality after sepsis diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models that combined five and more features showed a macro area under the curve (AUC) of 0.85 to predict 90-day mortality after sepsis diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numerical AUC result and prediction target with matching wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models demonstrated a macro AUC of 0.86 to discriminate between Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacterial infections. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not report the stated AUC for Gram-positive vs Gram-negative discrimination; the specific discrimination-AUC claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Parameters associated with the cellular immune response contributed the most to models predictive of 90 day mortality. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Parameters associated with the cellular immune response contributed the most to models predictive of 90-day mortality, particularly the proportion of T cells among PBMCs. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same finding but adds a specific example (proportion of T cells among PBMCs), thus clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Frequencies of Vδ2 + γδ T cells had the most profound impact on the prediction of Gram-negative infections. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Frequencies of Vδ2+ γδ T cells had the most profound impact on the prediction of Gram-negative infections. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this specific immune-cell feature impact with equivalent wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings highlight the added value of measuring the proportion and activation patterns of conventional and unconventional T cells in the blood of sepsis patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our findings highlight the added value of measuring the proportion and activation patterns of conventional and unconventional T cells in the blood of sepsis patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding statement is carried over verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Greater femoral neck width (FNW) and hip axis length (HAL) were independently related to an increased risk of radiographic hip osteoarthritis (rHOA). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both FNW and HAL retained independent relationships with radiographic HOA after adjusting for age, sex, height, weight and other geometric parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the independent relationships and provides further detail on adjustment covariates, clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Greater diameter of the femoral head (DFH) appeared to be protective against radiographic hip osteoarthritis (rHOA). (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Greater DFH appeared to be protective against radiographic HOA. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the protective association wording for DFH with equivalent interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Only femoral neck width (FNW) was independently related to hospital diagnosed hip osteoarthritis (HESOA) and total hip replacement (THR). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Greater FNW was independently related to HESOA and THR. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same specific association between FNW and HESOA/THR.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The relationships between geometric parameters and hip osteoarthritis (HOA) showed little attenuation after adjustment for height and weight. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both FNW and HAL retained independent relationships with radiographic HOA after adjusting for age, sex, height, weight and other geometric parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly reports that relationships remained after adjustment for height and weight, providing a clearer statement consistent with the preprint's observation of little attenuation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Individualized Doser (IDoser) achieved significant improvements when loss values were compared to observed clinical practice and a selected literature benchmark. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Individualized Doser (IDoser) model achieved a net improvement of up to 31.97% in validation cases compared to clinical practice. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication quantifies the reported improvement (up to 31.97%), clarifying the magnitude of the preprint's reported gain.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This methodology constitutes a simple but effective method to bridge the gap between current clinical dosing policies and gold policies based on the true underlying and often unknown dose-response functions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: IDoser links drug dose to relevant covariates via a set of coefficients and includes a loss function to code needed assumptions and requirements. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the method's technical formulation (linking dose to covariates and a loss function), which clarifies how the method operationalizes the preprint's claim about bridging policy gaps.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Computational methods can and should exploit field knowledge to address weaknesses associated with clinical observational data. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Computational methods can help overcome challenges in optimizing drug dosages by leveraging field knowledge. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same point about leveraging field knowledge in computational approaches to dosing optimization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Loneliness is linked to wide-ranging physical and mental health problems, including increased rates of mortality. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Loneliness is linked to wide ranging physical and mental health problems, including increased rates of mortality. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this well-established link with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study proposed a method for continuous detection of loneliness using fully objective data from smart devices and passive mobile sensing. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We found support for loneliness prediction using a multi-device and fully-objective approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication moves from proposing a method to reporting empirical support for prediction with the fully-objective, multi-device approach, clarifying and providing evidence for the original contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Behavioral data collected by smartphones were generally the most important features across all participants in predicting loneliness. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Behavioral data collected by smartphones generally were the most important features across all participants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same feature-importance finding with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides promising results for using objective data to monitor mental health indicators. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides promising results for using objective data to monitor mental health indicators. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding assessment is preserved in the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two different clusters of Major Depressive Disorder (MDD) patients were identified, differentiable with 67% accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two different clusters of Major Depressive Disorder (MDD) patients were identified, differentiable with 67% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same clustering result but presents it with higher certainty, increasing confidence in the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: One cluster of patients was associated with a higher proportion of treatment-resistant depression (TRD) and higher scores of energy-related depressive symptoms, childhood abuse, and emotional neglect. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: One cluster of patients was associated with a higher proportion of treatment-resistant depression (TRD) and higher scores of energy-related depressive symptoms, childhood abuse, and emotional neglect. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this specific cluster association verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The first cluster showed a widespread reduction in cortical thickness and volumes, along with fractional anisotropy in specific brain regions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The first cluster showed a widespread reduction in cortical thickness and volumes, along with fractional anisotropy in specific brain regions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the neuroimaging finding for the first cluster with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The second cluster was associated with cognitive and affective depressive symptoms and exhibited thicker cortices and wider volumes compared to the first cluster. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the second-cluster morphology (thicker cortices/wider volumes) as stated in the preprint; instead it introduces different brain–inflammation relationships.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The stratification of MDD patients based on structural neuroimaging can inform personalized and more effective interventions for treatment resistance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The stratification of MDD patients based on structural neuroimaging can contribute to the development of tailored personalized interventions for MDD. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the translational implication but phrases it as contributing to development of tailored interventions, slightly clarifying the expected impact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Three clusters of vaccine uptake and countermeasures were identified across Canadian provinces and territories.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Three clusters of vaccine uptake and countermeasures were identified, each with different levels of VOC prevalence.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the three clusters and adds that they differ in VOC prevalence, which refines but does not contradict the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The overall and variant-specific prevalence of VOCs was significantly different across the identified clusters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The overall and variant-specific prevalences of VOCs were significantly different across the identified clusters.
alignments.rationale: This publication claim is essentially identical in content and strength to the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Implementation of COVID-19 public health measures varied across the provinces and territories in Canada.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implementation of COVID-19 public health measures, including mass vaccination, is key to controlling VOC prevalence rates in Canada.
alignments.rationale: The preprint described variation in implementation; the publication reframes this into a stronger, prescriptive claim about the importance of measures (including vaccination) for controlling VOC prevalence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Completing the second dose of COVID-19 vaccine in a timely manner is crucial given the high prevalence of VOCs in Canada.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not make a specific claim about timely completion of second doses; the specific recommendation is not present in the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 4.4% of the cohort carried variants of clinical significance (VCSs) and 11.3% carried variants of uncertain significance (VUSs).
alignments.rationale: The publication list does not report these specific prevalence percentages, so this numerical result is not represented.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individuals with VCS or VUS tended to have limited improvements from interventions compared to non-carriers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individuals with VCS or VUS had improved significantly less after standard care than non-carriers at post-intervention.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same finding with stronger language (statistical significance) and specifies post-intervention timing, clarifying and amplifying the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Autistic individuals with likely pathogenic rare variants identified by exome sequencing might benefit less from standard care.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individuals with VCS or VUS had improved significantly less after standard care than non-carriers at post-intervention.
alignments.rationale: The publication documents reduced benefit for carriers (VCS/VUS), which corresponds to the preprint's suggestion about likely pathogenic rare variants but uses stronger, statistically stated language.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning model demonstrated the importance of rare variants for predicting intervention outcomes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our machine learning model showed the importance of rare variants for outcome prediction.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert the same result about the ML model identifying the importance of rare variants for prediction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Social Skills Group Training (SSGT) could be of heightened importance for autistic individuals with rare variants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Higher rare variant genetic scores for synaptic transmission and regulation of transcription from RNA polymerase II were separately associated with less beneficial or more beneficial effects after SSGT compared with standard care.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies how rare-variant genetic scores relate to differential effects of SSGT versus standard care, providing a more detailed and specific characterization of the preprint's more general suggestion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Seizure probability changes with day-to-day variations in sleep duration.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Seizure probability changes with day-to-day variations in sleep duration.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint claim verbatim and with the same strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An increase in sleep duration, by 1·66 ± 0·52 hours, lowered the odds of seizure by 27% in the following 48 hours.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An increase in sleep duration, by 1.66 ± 0.52 h, lowers the odds of seizure by 27% in the following 48 h.
alignments.rationale: The numeric effect size and conclusion are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Day-to-day deviations from regular sleep duration correlate with changes in seizure probability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Day-to-day deviations from regular sleep duration correlate with changes in seizure probability.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the conclusion but reports it with somewhat lower stated certainty (medium vs high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The occurrence of a seizure may disrupt sleep patterns by elongating sleep and reducing its quality if the seizure occurs during sleep.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The occurrence of a seizure may disrupt sleep patterns by elongating sleep and reducing its quality if the seizure occurs during sleep.
alignments.rationale: This conclusion appears unchanged between preprint and publication in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There was no quantitative evidence for negative impacts of online consultations (OCs) on patient safety.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There was no quantitative evidence for negative impacts of online consultations on patient safety.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint finding about lack of quantitative evidence on patient safety.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Staff workload decreased when sufficient resources were allocated to implement OCs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Online consultations can have negative impacts such as increased workload, which can be mitigated through appropriate system design and integration into organizational workflows.
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized workload decreases with adequate resources; the publication emphasizes potential increased workload as a negative impact and focuses on mitigation, reframing the finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patient satisfaction increased when using OCs due to better primary care access.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patient satisfaction increased when using online consultations due to better primary care access, although it decreased when using multiple-choice questionnaire formats.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the caveat that satisfaction falls with multiple-choice questionnaire formats, clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: OC systems that required patients to describe their queries using multiple choice questionnaires increased workload for both patients and staff.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patient satisfaction increased when using online consultations due to better primary care access, although it decreased when using multiple-choice questionnaire formats.
alignments.rationale: The publication highlights decreased satisfaction with MCQ formats but does not explicitly attribute increased workload to these formats; the claim is therefore reframed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study contributes new knowledge that OCs can produce both positive outcomes, such as increased access and patient satisfaction, and negative outcomes, such as increased workload and costs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study synthesizes research on online consultations in primary care and contributes new knowledge regarding their impacts on care quality.
alignments.rationale: Both statements describe the study's contribution to understanding both positive and negative impacts of online consultations; the publication restates this as the synthesis contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: T1-w might be a good candidate as a surrogate of contrast-enhanced sequence for the ChP manual segmentation task to estimate ChP volume.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Non-CE T1w sequences can be a surrogate of CE-T1w sequences for manual segmentation of choroid plexus.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same surrogate claim about non-contrast T1w for manual segmentation as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FLAIR introduces a systematic overestimation bias in ChP volume estimation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FLAIR sequences showed a higher contrast-to-noise ratio than T1w sequences but overestimated the choroid plexus volume.
alignments.rationale: Both statements report that FLAIR overestimates ChP volume while noting higher contrast-to-noise.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Non-contrast-enhanced T1-w can replace contrast-enhanced T1-w for estimating Choroid Plexus Volume because the quantified error is acceptable.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantitative analyses suggest that T1w can be used as a surrogate to estimate choroid plexus volume.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a similar quantitative endorsement but with more measured wording; the claim is maintained though stated with somewhat less certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FLAIR has a higher contrast-to-noise ratio than T1-w but overestimates the Choroid Plexus Volume.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FLAIR sequences showed a higher contrast-to-noise ratio than T1w sequences but overestimated the choroid plexus volume.
alignments.rationale: This is the same result phrased equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The segmentation metrics revealed that non-contrast-enhanced sequences had comparable Dice.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantitative analyses suggest that T1w can be used as a surrogate to estimate choroid plexus volume.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports quantitative analyses supporting T1w as a surrogate, which likely encompasses segmentation metrics like Dice, but the publication claims do not explicitly state Dice values, so the preprint metric is reported more indirectly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Each measured biomarker added complementary information when predicting conversion to Alzheimer’s disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adding additional biomarkers may provide little value for patients with high hippocampal volume, while further examination may be necessary for those with low hippocampal volume.
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserted a general complementary benefit of each biomarker; the paper qualifies that benefit as conditional on hippocampal volume, which revises and narrows the original universal claim and tempers confidence in universal benefit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nonlinear interaction models significantly improved prediction performance for conversion to Alzheimer’s disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nonlinear interaction models significantly improved sensitivity for predicting progressive mild cognitive impairment compared to independent and additive models.
alignments.rationale: Both statements report that nonlinear-interaction models improve prediction; the publication clarifies the improvement as specifically in sensitivity versus independent/additive models, narrowing the performance dimension assessed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The interaction between hippocampal atrophy and amyloid-deposition influences progression to Alzheimer’s disease beyond their independent contributions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Statistical models of biomarkers that account for nonlinear interactions can improve predictions of progression from mild cognitive impairment to Alzheimer's disease.
alignments.rationale: The preprint makes a specific hippocampal–amyloid interaction claim; the publication generalizes to nonlinear biomarker interactions improving prediction, which clarifies and broadens the specific interaction into a general statement about interactions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Gaussian Processes framework allows for the visual examination of nonlinear interactions among biomarkers.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or repeat a claim about using Gaussian Processes for visual examination of interactions, so this preprint methodological/contribution claim appears omitted in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The incubation period of COVID-19 did not follow general incubation distributions such as lognormal, Weibull, and Gamma distributions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The incubation period of COVID-19 did not follow general distributions such as lognormal, Weibull, or Gamma.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same negative result about standard incubation distributions with essentially identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean and median of the incubation period of COVID-19 were estimated to be 5.84 and 5.0 days, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean and median incubation periods of COVID-19 were estimated as 5.84 days and 5.01 days, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The estimated mean and median are the same (minor numeric rounding difference), so the result is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a statistically significant difference in the incubation periods between groups aged 40 years and older and those younger than 40 years, with the former group having a longer incubation period and larger variance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a statistically significant difference in incubation periods between groups aged 40 years and older and those younger than 40 years.
alignments.rationale: Both versions report the same statistically significant age-group difference; the publication omits wording about larger variance but maintains the core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different quarantine times should be applied to different age groups due to their varying incubation periods.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the age-difference result but does not make the policy recommendation on differing quarantine durations, so this prescriptive claim is absent in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The incubation difference between males and females did not demonstrate statistical significance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The incubation period difference between males and females was not statistically significant.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same non-significant sex difference result with similar certainty and wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SVM model achieved an AUC value of 0.786, 0.799, and 86.6, indicating good prediction of change detection in COVID-19 outbreak risk.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SVM model achieved an AUC value of 0.786, 0.799, and 86.6 on March 20, March 29, and April 10 respectively, indicating good prediction of outbreak risk change detection.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same SVM AUC results and conclusion, adding dates but preserving the claim and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average growth rate for active COVID-19 cases in Fars Province was 1.26, compared to 1.13 in the country and the world.
alignments.rationale: The paper does not report these specific average growth-rate figures; it instead describes general trends and volatility, so the numeric-growth-rate claim appears omitted or de-emphasized.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results of the third-degree polynomial and ARMA models revealed an increasing trend for growth rate, demonstrating that extensive quarantines have been effective.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The third-degree polynomial and ARIMA models revealed an increasing trend in virus infestation in Fars Province, demonstrating that extensive quarantines have been effective.
alignments.rationale: Both versions report the same modelling result (increasing trend) and the same conclusion attributing effectiveness to extensive quarantines; ARMA/ARIMA terminology differs but the core claim is preserved.
