alignments.rationale: The publication shortens the conclusion to the core comparative finding (opposition broader than positive rhetoric), slightly removing the explicit claim about tools for health authorities, thus a minor clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Utilizing the accelerated protocol resulted in a 1.94x gain in imaging throughput over the Gold Standard protocol. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accelerated MRI protocol resulted in a 1.94 × gain in imaging throughput. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same throughput gain; wording is equivalent and the result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The minimum/maximum PSNR gains from the baseline and subject-specific image-denoising models were 1.18/11.68 and 1.04/13.15, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Subject-specific image-denoising improved image quality, with minimum/maximum PSNR gains measured in dB being 1.04/13.15. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the subject-specific model's PSNR gains but omits the baseline model statistics (1.18/11.68) from the preprint, narrowing the claim to the subject-specific result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The volumetric measurements of the hippocampus and amygdala from the Gold Standard and accelerated protocols were in excellent agreement, as measured by the intra-class correlation coefficient. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Statistical analysis of volumetric measurements of the hippocampus and amygdala showed that 27 locations were in excellent agreement between the GS and accelerated protocols. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a more specific description (27 locations in excellent agreement) clarifying the extent of agreement reported in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Accelerated brain imaging with the potential for Alzheimer's Disease imaging was demonstrated. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate the claimed demonstration of AD imaging potential; instead it adds other protocol-specific contributions, so the preprint's conclusion appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithms correctly predicted action to seek help at study-end in 66 to 70% of cases, reaching 75% classification accuracy at follow-up. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithms correctly predicted action to seek help at study end in 65.9% to 70.3% of cases, reaching 74.8% classification accuracy at follow-up. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports nearly identical performance ranges and follow-up accuracy with only minimal numeric rounding differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The most important features for classifications included the degree of hearing loss, its perceived consequences in daily life, attitude towards hearing aids, and the personality traits neuroticism and conscientiousness. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The most important features for classification included perceived consequences of hearing loss, attitude toward hearing aids, motivation to seek help, physical health, sensory sensitivity, neuroticism, and income. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's feature list differs from the preprint: some preprint features (degree of loss, conscientiousness) are omitted or de-emphasized while other features (motivation, physical health, sensory sensitivity, income) are added, indicating a revised and broader feature set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study contributes to the identification of individual characteristics that predict help-seeking in older individuals with self-perceived hearing loss. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study contributes to the identification of individual characteristics that predict help seeking in older individuals with self-reported hearing loss. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same contribution but slightly alters wording from 'self-perceived' to 'self-reported,' a minor clarification without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Suggestions for the implementation of an individual profiling algorithm and for targeted recommendations in hearing mHealth applications are derived. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Suggestions are made for implementing individual characteristics in an individual-profiling algorithm for mHealth hearing apps. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the implementation suggestions with slightly more focused wording on using individual characteristics in mHealth apps, a clarification of the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Longitudinal imaging data improved CVRD dynamic prediction compared to traditional risk factors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Longitudinal imaging data improved CVRD dynamic prediction compared to traditional risk factors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Echocardiography measured in early adulthood predicted midlife CVRD risks almost as well as Echocardiography measured 10-15 years later. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Echocardiography measured in early adulthood predicted midlife CVRD risks almost as well as Echo measured 10-15 years later. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same comparative predictive finding with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The most recent CT exam provided the most accurate prediction for short-term risk estimation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The most recent CT exam provided the most accurate prediction for short-term risk estimation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical result reported in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Brain MRI markers provided additional information that led to a slightly improved prediction for CVRD risk. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Brain MRI markers provided additional information that led to a slightly improved prediction for CVRD risk. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Longitudinal multimodal imaging data can improve CVRD dynamic prediction. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Longitudinal multimodal imaging data can improve CVRD dynamic prediction. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The non-invasive estimation of intracranial pressure (nICP) was achieved with 95% limits of agreement of 3.8 mmHg and a negligible bias. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The nICP estimation exhibited 95% limits of agreement of 3.8 mmHg with minimal bias and a correlation of 0.8254 with invasive ICP monitoring. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the limits-of-agreement and bias claim while adding the numeric correlation, clarifying the result rather than changing it.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieved a good correlation coefficient of 82.54% compared to standard clinical invasive ICP monitoring. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The nICP estimation exhibited 95% limits of agreement of 3.8 mmHg with minimal bias and a correlation of 0.8254 with invasive ICP monitoring. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same correlation (0.8254 ~ 82.54%) alongside the agreement metrics; the correlation claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ROC curve analysis showed good diagnostic capability with a sensitivity of 80% and specificity of 89%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ROC curve analysis showed strong diagnostic capability with 80% sensitivity and 89% specificity for the nICP sensor. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the ROC performance metrics verbatim, maintaining the diagnostic capability claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clinical evaluation of this novel optical nICP sensor demonstrated for the first time that it could estimate ICP non-invasively to an acceptable and clinically useful accuracy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The clinical evaluation of the optical nICP sensor revealed its ability to estimate ICP non-invasively with acceptable and clinically useful accuracy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the clinical evaluation conclusion with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BharatSim is an open-source agent-based modelling framework specifically designed for the Indian population. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BharatSim is an open-source agent-based modelling framework specifically designed for the Indian population. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Claims are identical in wording and scope between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BharatSim can simulate populations at multiple scales, from small communities to states, and supports simulations of up to 50 million individual agents. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BharatSim can simulate populations at multiple scales, from small communities to states. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication retains multi-scale simulation claim but omits the explicit "up to 50 million agents" capacity, narrowing the scope of the quantitative claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BharatSim allows disease modellers to address unique questions related to the COVID-19 pandemic in India, such as the safe reopening of schools and the impact of new variants on disease dynamics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BharatSim allows disease modellers to address questions unique to India, particularly in the context of the COVID-19 pandemic. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Core claim about addressing India-specific COVID-19 questions is preserved with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The synthetic population created by BharatSim incorporates various individual attributes, including age, gender, and health conditions, which enhances the granularity of simulations. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication does not repeat the attribute-listing claim; instead it emphasizes data/methods for synthetic population creation (a different focus), so the specific attribute claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identified two clinical subgroups of autism primarily differing in restrictive and repetitive behaviors (RRB). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identified two clinical subgroups of autism primarily differing in restrictive and repetitive behaviors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this finding with the same emphasis and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both identified subgroups showed increased homogeneity in localized neuroanatomical deviations compared to the entire autism group. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both identified subgroups showed increased neurobiological homogeneity in localized deviations compared to the entire autism group. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Content is effectively the same, but the publication expresses the claim with lower stated certainty, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The high-RRB subgroup exhibited increased volume deviations in the cerebellum, while the low-RRB subgroup showed decreased cortical thickness deviations in the postcentral gyrus and fusiform cortex. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The high-RRB subgroup exhibited increased volume deviations in the cerebellum, while the low-RRB subgroup showed decreased cortical thickness deviations in the postcentral gyrus and fusiform cortex. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Specific neuroanatomical findings are identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Despite the identification of clinical subgroups, substantial within-group heterogeneity remained, as indicated by the classifier's failure to distinguish between the subgroups. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Substantial within-group heterogeneity remained, as indicated by the lack of improvement in classifier performance when distinguishing between the subgroups and nonautistic individuals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication rephrases and broadens the classifier-related result (mentions performance versus nonautistic individuals) and states it with higher certainty, so the claim is revised, broadened and confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Identifying subgroups of autism has significant clinical implications, potentially leading to more tailored behavioral interventions and improved clinical outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate this clinical-implication framing; instead it issues a methodological future-research recommendation (added), so the original implication statement is not preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predictors of donor heart acceptance for transplantation have changed significantly over the last two decades. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predictors of donor heart acceptance for transplantation have changed significantly over the last 2 decades. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The overarching conclusion is the same, though the publication reports it with lower stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Older age has become increasingly predictive of discard over time while other factors related to drug use, infection, and mild cardiac diagnostic abnormalities have become less influential. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Older age has become increasingly predictive of discard over time among potential heart donors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication highlights the older-age component explicitly; the preprint bundled older age with other factor trends, so the publication clarifies this element.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A random forest model outperformed other prediction algorithms in predicting donor heart acceptance, achieving an area under the curve of 0.90 and accuracy of 0.82. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A random forest model outperformed other prediction algorithms in predicting donor heart acceptance, achieving an area under the curve of 0.908 and accuracy of 0.831. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication reports the same model performance with slightly more precise metric values, clarifying the numeric results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The web-based prediction tool developed from the random forest model may improve efficiency during donor management and heart allocation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Real-time prediction of donor heart acceptance using the web-based tool may improve efficiency during donor management and heart allocation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The practical conclusion about the web tool's potential to improve efficiency is preserved with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Factors related to drug use, infection, and mild cardiac diagnostic abnormalities have become less influential in predicting donor heart acceptance over time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly separates trends for specific non-age factors into its own result; while those trends were mentioned within a composite preprint claim, the publication presents this as a distinct claim (treated here as an added explicit claim).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified 32 eligible systematic reviews, of which only seven described the development and validation of risk prediction tools for pressure injuries. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified 32 eligible systematic reviews, of which only seven described the development and validation of risk prediction tools for pressure injuries. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical reporting of the screening result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The majority of available risk prediction tools for pressure injuries have not been externally validated. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The majority of risk prediction tools for pressure injuries have not been externally validated. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Core conclusion preserved between versions without change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All models but one assessed for risk of bias using the PROBAST tool were found to be at high or unclear risk of bias. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: All models but one assessed for risk of bias using the PROBAST tool were found to be at high or unclear risk of bias. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Statement about PROBAST-assessed risk of bias is identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Available tools do not meet current standards for the development or reporting of risk prediction models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Available tools do not meet current standards for the development or reporting of risk prediction models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion about standards compliance is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Standardised and rigorous approaches to risk prediction model development and validation are needed. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Standardised and rigorous approaches to risk prediction model development and validation are needed. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The recommendation is preserved; publication labels it as a contribution rather than a conclusion, but the content and certainty are the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A host-RNA signature was identified and validated as a biomarker for individualized therapy durations for patients with multidrug-resistant tuberculosis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A host-RNA signature has been identified and validated as a biomarker for individualised therapy durations for patients with drug-susceptible and multidrug-resistant tuberculosis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication expands the applicability to both drug-susceptible and multidrug-resistant TB, broadening the claim while keeping validation status.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 22-gene RNA model accurately predicted clinical outcomes for patients with drug-susceptible tuberculosis in the validation cohort. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TB22 model was superior to other published signatures in accurately predicting clinical outcomes for patients with drug-susceptible tuberculosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication not only reports accurate prediction but also asserts superiority over other signatures, strengthening the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cure may be achieved with shorter treatment durations for tuberculosis patients with multidrug-resistant tuberculosis, with mean reductions of 218.0 days, 211.0 days, and 161.0 days in different cohorts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cure may be achieved with shorter treatment durations for tuberculosis patients with multidrug-resistant strains, with mean reductions of 218.0 days, 211.0 days, and 161.0 days observed in different cohorts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Quantitative treatment-reduction findings are preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Biomarker-guided management may substantially shorten the duration of therapy for many patients with multidrug-resistant tuberculosis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Biomarker-guided management may substantially shorten the duration of therapy for many patients with multidrug-resistant tuberculosis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about the potential of biomarker-guided management is unchanged in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our analysis reveals four significant clusters of T2DM patients, with two clusters comprising mainly of non-obese individuals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our analysis reveals four significant clusters of T2DM patients, with two clusters comprising mainly of non-obese individuals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication claim are effectively identical in content and certainty describing the same clustering result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The non-obese clusters have a lower mean age and are predominantly composed of rural residents. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The non-obese clusters have a lower mean age and are primarily composed of rural residents. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same demographic characteristics with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: One of the obese clusters contains 90% of T2DM patients practicing a non-vegetarian diet, despite not showing an increased intake of plant-based protein-rich foods. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: One of the obese clusters contains 90% of T2DM patients practicing a non-vegetarian diet without increased intake of plant-based protein-rich foods. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this specific dietary-cluster result with minimal wording change and same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings demonstrate the presence of heterogeneity among T2DM patients regarding socio-demography and dietary patterns. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The existence of significant non-obese T2DM sub-populations characterized by younger age groups and economic disadvantage necessitates different screening criteria for T2DM among rural Indian residents. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the general statement of heterogeneity into a more specific identification of sub-populations and policy implications, thereby clarifying and narrowing the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These results support the development of demography-specific screening criteria and lifestyle modification guidelines for T2DM. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The existence of significant non-obese T2DM sub-populations characterized by younger age groups and economic disadvantage necessitates different screening criteria for T2DM among rural Indian residents. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the recommendation for demography-specific screening but makes it more specific (rural Indian residents) and states it with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ML models predicted the risk of death with a ROC-AUC of 0.904 at diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ML models predicted the risk of death with a ROC-AUC of 0.906 at diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions report the same model performance metric at diagnosis; the small numeric difference is negligible and does not alter the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Common risk factors identified include age, body mass index (BMI), and hypertension. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Common risk factors for severe COVID-19 included age, body mass index, and hypertension. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The same risk factors are reported in the publication but with stronger stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The external validation indicated fair predictive performance for mortality prediction but suboptimal performance for predicting ICU admission. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The external validation indicated fair predictive performance for mortality prediction but suboptimal performance for predicting ICU admission. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The external validation results are reported identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Markers of shock and organ dysfunction became more important in more severe cases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ML may be used to identify drivers of progression to more severe disease and for prognostication in patients with COVID-19. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The specific observation about markers is generalized in the publication to a broader claim about ML's utility in identifying progression drivers and prognostication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An online risk calculator based on these findings has been provided for use. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or mention the availability of an online risk calculator present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Coherent Voting Network (CVN) methodology demonstrates high predictive values for post-surgery breast cancer outcomes based on small molecular fingerprints. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methodology predicts 5-years survival for breast cancer patients with improved accuracy over state-of-the-art prediction methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the performance specifically as 5-year survival prediction and emphasizes improvement over state-of-the-art, narrowing the original broader phrasing about post-surgery outcomes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For lymph node positive patients, the CVN achieves a positive predictive value (PPV) of 0.77 and a negative predictive value (NPV) of 0.78. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's summary does not include these specific PPV/NPV performance numbers for lymph node positive patients.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the testing pool of triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) patients, the CVN achieves a PPV of 1.0 and an NPV of 0.83. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report these specific TNBC testing pool performance figures in the presented claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of the selected molecular fingerprints is confirmed on several independent data sets from the NCBI Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's highlighted claims do not mention independent GEO validation despite the preprint stating confirmation on several external datasets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Repetitive blast exposure potentiated ethanol-induced locomotor stimulation and shifted ethanol intake patterns in a mouse model of blast mTBI. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Only repetitive blast potentiated EtOH-induced locomotor stimulation and shifted EtOH intake patterns. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Veterans with a history of blast exposure had increased AUDIT-C scores, indicating higher alcohol consumption behaviors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is an increased potential for adverse health risk behaviors following repetitive blast-mTBI. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cluster analysis revealed that Veterans with a history of blast mTBI shifted from a 'frequent' to a 'risky' drinking pattern compared to those without a history of TBI. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Veterans with a history of blast-mTBI displayed a shift in cluster assignment from 'frequent' to 'risky' in AUDIT-C scores compared to Veterans without a history of TBI. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: These results offer new insight into how blast-mTBI may increase AUD risk. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
